1→package com.secman.domain
     2→
     3→import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable
     4→import jakarta.persistence.*
     5→import jakarta.validation.constraints.Email
     6→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotBlank
     7→import jakarta.validation.constraints.Pattern
     8→import java.time.Instant
     9→
    10→/**
    11→ * UserMapping entity - stores mappings between user emails, AWS account IDs, domains, and IP addresses
    12→ *
    13→ * Features: 013-user-mapping-upload, 020-i-want-to (IP address mapping), 042-future-user-mappings
    14→ * Purpose: Enable role-based access control across multiple AWS accounts, domains, and IP addresses
    15→ * Now supports future user mappings - mappings for users who don't yet exist in the system
    16→ *
    17→ * Business Rules:
    18→ * - Email is REQUIRED
    19→ * - At least one of AWS Account ID, Domain, or IP Address must be provided
    20→ * - One email can map to multiple AWS accounts (many-to-many)
    21→ * - One email can map to multiple domains (many-to-many)
    22→ * - One email can map to multiple IP addresses/ranges (many-to-many)
    23→ * - Unique constraint on (email, awsAccountId, domain, ipAddress) prevents duplicates
    24→ * - Email and domain are normalized to lowercase for case-insensitive matching
    25→ * - AWS account IDs must be exactly 12 numeric digits when provided
    26→ * - IP addresses support three formats: single (192.168.1.100), CIDR (192.168.1.0/24), dash range (192.168.1.1-192.168.1.100)
    27→ * - User reference is nullable - allows future user mappings (Feature 042)
    28→ * - AppliedAt tracks when future user mapping was applied to a user (Feature 042)
    29→ *
    30→ * Example Mappings:
    31→ * - john@example.com → 123456789012 → null → null (email + AWS account only)
    32→ * - john@example.com → null → example.com → null (email + domain only)
    33→ * - john@example.com → null → null → 192.168.1.0/24 (email + IP range)
    34→ * - john@example.com → 123456789012 → example.com → 192.168.1.0/24 (email + AWS + domain + IP)
    35→ * - future@example.com → 123456789012 → null → null + user=null + appliedAt=null (future user mapping)
    36→ *
    37→ * Related to: Feature 013 (User Mapping Upload), Feature 020 (IP Address Mapping), Feature 042 (Future User Mappings)
    38→ */
    39→@Entity
    40→@Table(
    41→    name = "user_mapping",
    42→    uniqueConstraints = [
    43→        UniqueConstraint(
    44→            name = "uk_user_mapping_composite",
    45→            columnNames = ["email", "aws_account_id", "domain", "ip_address"]
    46→        )
    47→    ],
    48→    indexes = [
    49→        Index(name = "idx_user_mapping_email", columnList = "email"),
    50→        Index(name = "idx_user_mapping_aws_account", columnList = "aws_account_id"),
    51→        Index(name = "idx_user_mapping_domain", columnList = "domain"),
    52→        Index(name = "idx_user_mapping_email_aws", columnList = "email,aws_account_id"),
    53→        Index(name = "idx_user_mapping_ip_address", columnList = "ip_address"),
    54→        Index(name = "idx_user_mapping_ip_range", columnList = "ip_range_start,ip_range_end"),
    55→        Index(name = "idx_user_mapping_email_ip", columnList = "email,ip_address"),
    56→        Index(name = "idx_user_mapping_applied_at", columnList = "applied_at"), // Feature 042: Efficient filtering for Current vs Applied History tabs
    57→        Index(name = "idx_user_mapping_status", columnList = "status"), // Feature 049: Status filtering
    58→        Index(name = "idx_user_mapping_email_status", columnList = "email,status") // Feature 049: User + status queries
    59→    ]
    60→)
    61→@Serdeable
    62→data class UserMapping(
    63→    @Id
    64→    @GeneratedValue(strategy = GenerationType.IDENTITY)
    65→    var id: Long? = null,
    66→
    67→    @Column(nullable = false, length = 255)
    68→    @Email(message = "Invalid email format")
    69→    @NotBlank(message = "Email address is required")
    70→    var email: String,
    71→
    72→    // Feature 042: User reference (nullable for future user mappings)
    73→    @ManyToOne(fetch = FetchType.LAZY, optional = true)
    74→    @JoinColumn(name = "user_id", nullable = true)
    75→    var user: User? = null,
    76→
    77→    // Feature 042: Timestamp when mapping was applied to user (null = not yet applied)
    78→    @Column(name = "applied_at", nullable = true)
    79→    var appliedAt: Instant? = null,
    80→
    81→    @Column(name = "aws_account_id", nullable = true, length = 12)
    82→    @Pattern(regexp = "^\\d{12}$", message = "AWS Account ID must be exactly 12 numeric digits")
    83→    var awsAccountId: String?,
    84→
    85→    @Column(nullable = true, length = 255)
    86→    @Pattern(regexp = "^[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+$", message = "Domain must contain only letters, numbers, dots, and hyphens")
    87→    var domain: String?,
    88→
    89→    // IP Address Mapping Fields (Feature 020)
    90→    @Column(name = "ip_address", nullable = true, length = 100)
    91→    var ipAddress: String? = null,
    92→
    93→    @Column(name = "ip_range_type", nullable = true, length = 20)
    94→    @Enumerated(EnumType.STRING)
    95→    var ipRangeType: IpRangeType? = null,
    96→
    97→    @Column(name = "ip_range_start", nullable = true)
    98→    var ipRangeStart: Long? = null,
    99→
   100→    @Column(name = "ip_range_end", nullable = true)
   101→    var ipRangeEnd: Long? = null,
   102→
   103→    @Column(name = "created_at", updatable = false)
   104→    var createdAt: Instant? = null,
   105→
   106→    @Column(name = "updated_at")
   107→    var updatedAt: Instant? = null,
   108→
   109→    // Feature 049: Status tracking for pending vs active mappings
   110→    @Column(name = "status", nullable = false, length = 20)
   111→    @Enumerated(EnumType.STRING)
   112→    var status: MappingStatus = MappingStatus.ACTIVE
   113→) {
   114→    /**
   115→     * Lifecycle callback - executed before entity is persisted to database
   116→     * Normalizes email and domain to lowercase, trims whitespace
   117→     */
   118→    @PrePersist
   119→    fun onCreate() {
   120→        val now = Instant.now()
   121→        createdAt = now
   122→        updatedAt = now
   123→        // Normalize email and domain to lowercase for case-insensitive matching
   124→        email = email.lowercase().trim()
   125→        domain = domain?.lowercase()?.trim()
   126→        awsAccountId = awsAccountId?.trim()
   127→
   128→        // Feature 049: Set status based on user existence
   129→        // PENDING if user is null (future user mapping), ACTIVE otherwise
   130→        if (user == null && status == MappingStatus.ACTIVE) {
   131→            status = MappingStatus.PENDING
   132→        }
   133→    }
   134→
   135→    /**
   136→     * Lifecycle callback - executed before entity is updated in database
   137→     * Updates the updatedAt timestamp
   138→     */
   139→    @PreUpdate
   140→    fun onUpdate() {
   141→        updatedAt = Instant.now()
   142→    }
   143→
   144→    /**
   145→     * Feature 042: Returns true if this is a future user mapping (not yet applied)
   146→     * A future mapping has no user reference and no appliedAt timestamp
   147→     */
   148→    fun isFutureMapping(): Boolean {
   149→        return user == null && appliedAt == null
   150→    }
   151→
   152→    /**
   153→     * Feature 042: Returns true if this is an applied historical mapping
   154→     * An applied mapping has an appliedAt timestamp (regardless of user reference)
   155→     */
   156→    fun isAppliedMapping(): Boolean {
   157→        return appliedAt != null
   158→    }
   159→
   160→    override fun toString(): String {
   161→        return "UserMapping(id=$id, email='$email', awsAccountId='$awsAccountId', domain='$domain', ipAddress='$ipAddress', ipRangeType=$ipRangeType)"
   162→    }
   163→
   164→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean {
   165→        if (this === other) return true
   166→        if (other !is UserMapping) return false
   167→        return id != null && id == other.id
   168→    }
   169→
   170→    override fun hashCode(): Int {
   171→        return id?.hashCode() ?: 0
   172→    }
   173→}
   174→

1→package com.secman.domain
     2→
     3→import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable
     4→import jakarta.persistence.*
     5→import java.time.Instant
     6→
     7→/**
     8→ * AwsAccountSharing entity - stores directional, non-transitive sharing rules
     9→ * for AWS account visibility between users.
    10→ *
    11→ * Feature: AWS Account Sharing
    12→ *
    13→ * Semantics:
    14→ * - sourceUser shares their AWS account visibility with targetUser
    15→ * - targetUser can then see all assets whose cloudAccountId matches
    16→ *   any of sourceUser's AWS account mappings (via user_mapping)
    17→ * - Sharing is directional: A->B does NOT imply B->A
    18→ * - Sharing is NOT transitive: if A->B and B->C, C does NOT see A's accounts via B
    19→ *
    20→ * Business Rules:
    21→ * - sourceUser and targetUser must be different users
    22→ * - Unique constraint on (source_user_id, target_user_id) prevents duplicates
    23→ * - Only ADMIN users can create/manage sharing rules
    24→ * - Application-level cleanup in UserController.delete() removes sharing rules before user deletion
    25→ * - createdBy tracks which admin created the rule for audit purposes
    26→ */
    27→@Entity
    28→@Table(
    29→    name = "aws_account_sharing",
    30→    uniqueConstraints = [
    31→        UniqueConstraint(
    32→            name = "uk_aws_sharing_source_target",
    33→            columnNames = ["source_user_id", "target_user_id"]
    34→        )
    35→    ],
    36→    indexes = [
    37→        Index(name = "idx_aws_sharing_source", columnList = "source_user_id"),
    38→        Index(name = "idx_aws_sharing_target", columnList = "target_user_id"),
    39→        Index(name = "idx_aws_sharing_created_by", columnList = "created_by_id")
    40→    ]
    41→)
    42→@Serdeable
    43→data class AwsAccountSharing(
    44→    @Id
    45→    @GeneratedValue(strategy = GenerationType.IDENTITY)
    46→    var id: Long? = null,
    47→
    48→    @ManyToOne(fetch = FetchType.LAZY, optional = false)
    49→    @JoinColumn(name = "source_user_id", nullable = false)
    50→    var sourceUser: User,
    51→
    52→    @ManyToOne(fetch = FetchType.LAZY, optional = false)
    53→    @JoinColumn(name = "target_user_id", nullable = false)
    54→    var targetUser: User,
    55→
    56→    @ManyToOne(fetch = FetchType.LAZY, optional = false)
    57→    @JoinColumn(name = "created_by_id", nullable = false)
    58→    var createdBy: User,
    59→
    60→    @Column(name = "created_at", updatable = false)
    61→    var createdAt: Instant? = null,
    62→
    63→    @Column(name = "updated_at")
    64→    var updatedAt: Instant? = null
    65→) {
    66→    @PrePersist
    67→    fun onCreate() {
    68→        val now = Instant.now()
    69→        createdAt = now
    70→        updatedAt = now
    71→    }
    72→
    73→    @PreUpdate
    74→    fun onUpdate() {
    75→        updatedAt = Instant.now()
    76→    }
    77→
    78→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean {
    79→        if (this === other) return true
    80→        if (other !is AwsAccountSharing) return false
    81→        return id != null && id == other.id
    82→    }
    83→
    84→    override fun hashCode(): Int = id?.hashCode() ?: 0
    85→
    86→    override fun toString(): String {
    87→        return "AwsAccountSharing(id=$id, sourceUserId=${sourceUser.id}, targetUserId=${targetUser.id})"
    88→    }
    89→}
    90→

1→package com.secman.repository
     2→
     3→import com.secman.domain.UserMapping
     4→import io.micronaut.data.annotation.Query
     5→import io.micronaut.data.annotation.Repository
     6→import io.micronaut.data.jpa.repository.JpaRepository
     7→import java.util.*
     8→
     9→/**
    10→ * Repository for UserMapping entity
    11→ * 
    12→ * Feature: 013-user-mapping-upload
    13→ * 
    14→ * Provides CRUD operations and query methods for user-to-AWS-account-to-domain mappings.
    15→ * Used by UserMappingImportService for bulk imports and by future RBAC features
    16→ * for access control lookups.
    17→ * 
    18→ * Related to: Feature 013 (User Mapping Upload)
    19→ */
    20→@Repository
    21→interface UserMappingRepository : JpaRepository<UserMapping, Long> {
    22→
    23→    /**
    24→     * Find all mappings for a specific email address
    25→     * 
    26→     * Use case: Get all AWS accounts and domains a user has access to
    27→     * 
    28→     * @param email User's email address (case-insensitive, will be normalized)
    29→     * @return List of mappings for the email
    30→     */
    31→    fun findByEmail(email: String): List<UserMapping>
    32→
    33→    /**
    34→     * Find all mappings for a specific AWS account
    35→     * 
    36→     * Use case: Get all users with access to an AWS account
    37→     * 
    38→     * @param awsAccountId AWS account identifier (12-digit string)
    39→     * @return List of mappings for the AWS account
    40→     */
    41→    fun findByAwsAccountId(awsAccountId: String): List<UserMapping>
    42→
    43→    /**
    44→     * Find all mappings for a specific domain
    45→     * 
    46→     * Use case: Get all users within an organizational domain
    47→     * 
    48→     * @param domain Organizational domain name
    49→     * @return List of mappings for the domain
    50→     */
    51→    fun findByDomain(domain: String): List<UserMapping>
    52→
    53→    /**
    54→     * Check if a specific mapping exists (duplicate detection)
    55→     *
    56→     * Use case: Skip duplicate mappings during Excel import
    57→     *
    58→     * @param email User's email address (required)
    59→     * @param awsAccountId AWS account identifier (nullable)
    60→     * @param domain Organizational domain name (nullable)
    61→     * @return true if mapping exists, false otherwise
    62→     */
    63→    fun existsByEmailAndAwsAccountIdAndDomain(
    64→        email: String,
    65→        awsAccountId: String?,
    66→        domain: String?
    67→    ): Boolean
    68→
    69→    /**
    70→     * Find a specific mapping by composite key
    71→     *
    72→     * Use case: Retrieve mapping for update or verification
    73→     *
    74→     * @param email User's email address (required)
    75→     * @param awsAccountId AWS account identifier (nullable)
    76→     * @param domain Organizational domain name (nullable)
    77→     * @return Optional containing the mapping if found
    78→     */
    79→    fun findByEmailAndAwsAccountIdAndDomain(
    80→        email: String,
    81→        awsAccountId: String?,
    82→        domain: String?
    83→    ): Optional<UserMapping>
    84→
    85→    /**
    86→     * Count total mappings for a user
    87→     * 
    88→     * Use case: Display user's total access scope
    89→     * 
    90→     * @param email User's email address
    91→     * @return Number of mappings for the user
    92→     */
    93→    fun countByEmail(email: String): Long
    94→
    95→    /**
    96→     * Count total mappings for an AWS account
    97→     * 
    98→     * Use case: Display how many users have access to an account
    99→     * 
   100→     * @param awsAccountId AWS account identifier
   101→     * @return Number of mappings for the account
   102→     */
   103→    fun countByAwsAccountId(awsAccountId: String): Long
   104→
   105→    /**
   106→     * Find distinct AWS account IDs for a user
   107→     *
   108→     * Use case: Get list of AWS accounts a user can access
   109→     *
   110→     * @param email User's email address
   111→     * @return List of distinct AWS account IDs (excluding null values)
   112→     */
   113→    @Query("SELECT DISTINCT m.awsAccountId FROM UserMapping m WHERE m.email = :email AND m.awsAccountId IS NOT NULL")
   114→    fun findDistinctAwsAccountIdByEmail(email: String): List<String>
   115→
   116→    @Query("SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT m.awsAccountId) FROM UserMapping m WHERE m.email = :email AND m.awsAccountId IS NOT NULL")
   117→    fun countDistinctAwsAccountsByEmail(email: String): Long
   118→
   119→    /**
   120→     * Find distinct domains for a user
   121→     *
   122→     * Use case: Get list of domains a user has access to
   123→     *
   124→     * @param email User's email address
   125→     * @return List of distinct domains (excluding null values)
   126→     */
   127→    @Query("SELECT DISTINCT m.domain FROM UserMapping m WHERE m.email = :email AND m.domain IS NOT NULL")
   128→    fun findDistinctDomainByEmail(email: String): List<String>
   129→
   130→    @Query("SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT m.domain) FROM UserMapping m WHERE m.email = :email AND m.domain IS NOT NULL")
   131→    fun countDistinctDomainsByEmail(email: String): Long
   132→
   133→    @Query("SELECT DISTINCT m.domain FROM UserMapping m WHERE m.domain IS NOT NULL AND m.domain != ''")
   134→    fun findDistinctDomains(): List<String>
   135→
   136→    // IP Address Mapping - Feature 020
   137→
   138→    /**
   139→     * Find all IP address mappings for a specific email
   140→     *
   141→     * Use case: Get all IP ranges a user has access to
   142→     *
   143→     * Related to: Feature 020 (IP Address Mapping)
   144→     *
   145→     * @param email User's email address
   146→     * @return List of mappings with IP addresses (excluding null IP addresses)
   147→     */
   148→    @Query("SELECT m FROM UserMapping m WHERE m.email = :email AND m.ipAddress IS NOT NULL")
   149→    fun findIpMappingsByEmail(email: String): List<UserMapping>
   150→
   151→    /**
   152→     * Check if a specific IP mapping exists (duplicate detection)
   153→     *
   154→     * Use case: Skip duplicate IP mappings during CSV/Excel import
   155→     *
   156→     * Related to: Feature 020 (IP Address Mapping)
   157→     *
   158→     * @param email User's email address (required)
   159→     * @param ipAddress IP address string (nullable)
   160→     * @param domain Organizational domain name (nullable)
   161→     * @return true if mapping exists, false otherwise
   162→     */
   163→    fun existsByEmailAndIpAddressAndDomain(
   164→        email: String,
   165→        ipAddress: String?,
   166→        domain: String?
   167→    ): Boolean
   168→
   169→    /**
   170→     * Find a specific IP mapping by composite key
   171→     *
   172→     * Use case: Retrieve IP mapping for update or verification
   173→     *
   174→     * Related to: Feature 020 (IP Address Mapping)
   175→     *
   176→     * @param email User's email address (required)
   177→     * @param ipAddress IP address string (nullable)
   178→     * @param domain Organizational domain name (nullable)
   179→     * @return Optional containing the mapping if found
   180→     */
   181→    fun findByEmailAndIpAddressAndDomain(
   182→        email: String,
   183→        ipAddress: String?,
   184→        domain: String?
   185→    ): Optional<UserMapping>
   186→
   187→    /**
   188→     * Count total IP mappings for a user
   189→     *
   190→     * Use case: Display user's IP-based access scope
   191→     *
   192→     * Related to: Feature 020 (IP Address Mapping)
   193→     *
   194→     * @param email User's email address
   195→     * @return Number of IP mappings for the user
   196→     */
   197→    @Query("SELECT COUNT(m) FROM UserMapping m WHERE m.email = :email AND m.ipAddress IS NOT NULL")
   198→    fun countIpMappingsByEmail(email: String): Long
   199→
   200→    // Future User Mapping - Feature 042
   201→
   202→    /**
   203→     * Find mapping by email (case-insensitive)
   204→     *
   205→     * Use case: Lookup future user mapping during user creation for automatic application
   206→     *
   207→     * Related to: Feature 042 (Future User Mappings)
   208→     *
   209→     * @param email User's email address (case-insensitive)
   210→     * @return Optional containing the first matching mapping (if multiple exist, returns first)
   211→     */
   212→    fun findByEmailIgnoreCase(email: String): Optional<UserMapping>
   213→
   214→    /**
   215→     * Find all current mappings (future + active, excluding applied history)
   216→     *
   217→     * Use case: Display "Current Mappings" tab in UI (paginated)
   218→     *
   219→     * Related to: Feature 042 (Future User Mappings)
   220→     *
   221→     * @param pageable Pagination parameters (page number, size, sort)
   222→     * @return Page of current mappings (appliedAt IS NULL)
   223→     */
   224→    fun findByAppliedAtIsNull(pageable: io.micronaut.data.model.Pageable): io.micronaut.data.model.Page<UserMapping>
   225→
   226→    /**
   227→     * Find all applied historical mappings
   228→     *
   229→     * Use case: Display "Applied History" tab in UI (paginated)
   230→     *
   231→     * Related to: Feature 042 (Future User Mappings)
   232→     *
   233→     * @param pageable Pagination parameters (page number, size, sort)
   234→     * @return Page of applied historical mappings (appliedAt IS NOT NULL)
   235→     */
   236→    fun findByAppliedAtIsNotNull(pageable: io.micronaut.data.model.Pageable): io.micronaut.data.model.Page<UserMapping>
   237→
   238→    /**
   239→     * Count current mappings (future + active)
   240→     *

1→package com.secman.repository
     2→
     3→import com.secman.domain.AwsAccountSharing
     4→import io.micronaut.data.annotation.Query
     5→import io.micronaut.data.annotation.Repository
     6→import io.micronaut.data.jpa.repository.JpaRepository
     7→import io.micronaut.data.model.Page
     8→import io.micronaut.data.model.Pageable
     9→import java.util.Optional
    10→
    11→/**
    12→ * Repository for AwsAccountSharing entity.
    13→ *
    14→ * Feature: AWS Account Sharing
    15→ */
    16→@Repository
    17→interface AwsAccountSharingRepository : JpaRepository<AwsAccountSharing, Long> {
    18→
    19→    fun findByTargetUserId(targetUserId: Long): List<AwsAccountSharing>
    20→
    21→    fun countByTargetUserId(targetUserId: Long): Long
    22→
    23→    fun findBySourceUserId(sourceUserId: Long): List<AwsAccountSharing>
    24→
    25→    fun deleteBySourceUserId(sourceUserId: Long)
    26→
    27→    fun deleteByTargetUserId(targetUserId: Long)
    28→
    29→    fun deleteByCreatedBy_Id(createdById: Long)
    30→
    31→    fun existsBySourceUserIdAndTargetUserId(sourceUserId: Long, targetUserId: Long): Boolean
    32→
    33→    fun findBySourceUserIdAndTargetUserId(sourceUserId: Long, targetUserId: Long): Optional<AwsAccountSharing>
    34→
    35→    fun deleteBySourceUserIdAndTargetUserId(sourceUserId: Long, targetUserId: Long)
    36→
    37→    override fun findAll(pageable: Pageable): Page<AwsAccountSharing>
    38→
    39→    /**
    40→     * Find all sharing rules with eager-loaded user associations for admin listing.
    41→     */
    42→    @Query("""
    43→        SELECT s FROM AwsAccountSharing s
    44→        LEFT JOIN FETCH s.sourceUser
    45→        LEFT JOIN FETCH s.targetUser
    46→        LEFT JOIN FETCH s.createdBy
    47→        ORDER BY s.createdAt DESC
    48→    """)
    49→    fun findAllWithUsers(): List<AwsAccountSharing>
    50→
    51→    /**
    52→     * Find all AWS account IDs accessible to a target user via sharing rules.
    53→     * Joins aws_account_sharing -> users -> user_mapping to resolve shared accounts.
    54→     *
    55→     * This is the critical query for access control integration.
    56→     * Non-transitive by design: only joins user_mapping (direct mappings),
    57→     * not recursively computed visibility.
    58→     */
    59→    @Query(
    60→        value = """
    61→            SELECT DISTINCT um.aws_account_id
    62→            FROM aws_account_sharing acs
    63→            JOIN users u_source ON u_source.id = acs.source_user_id
    64→            JOIN user_mapping um ON um.email = u_source.email AND um.aws_account_id IS NOT NULL
    65→            WHERE acs.target_user_id = :targetUserId
    66→        """,
    67→        nativeQuery = true
    68→    )
    69→    fun findSharedAwsAccountIdsByTargetUserId(targetUserId: Long): List<String>
    70→}
    71→

     3→/**
     4→ * Status enum for UserMapping entity (Feature 049)
     5→ *
     6→ * Tracks whether a mapping is:
     7→ * - PENDING: Created for a user that doesn't exist yet (future user mapping)
     8→ * - ACTIVE: Applied to an existing user
     9→ *
    10→ * State transition: PENDING → ACTIVE (when user is created)
    11→ * No reverse transition - mappings are deleted instead of reverting to PENDING
    12→ */
    13→enum class MappingStatus {
    14→    /**
    15→     * Mapping created for non-existent user
    16→     * Will be auto-applied when user is created via UserCreatedEvent
    17→     */
    18→    PENDING,
    19→
    20→    /**
    21→     * Mapping applied to existing user
    22→     * User can access assets based on this mapping
    23→     */
    24→    ACTIVE
    25→}

1→package com.secman.domain
     2→
     3→import com.fasterxml.jackson.annotation.JsonIgnore
     4→import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable
     5→import jakarta.persistence.*
     6→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotNull
     7→import java.time.LocalDateTime
     8→
     9→/**
    10→ * Vulnerability entity representing a security vulnerability discovered during a scan.
    11→ *
    12→ * Linked to an Asset entity via ManyToOne relationship.
    13→ * Feature 048: Uses transactional replace pattern - each import deletes existing vulnerabilities
    14→ * for an asset and inserts current ones, ensuring only the latest state is stored.
    15→ *
    16→ * Related to: Feature 003-i-want-to (Vulnerability Management System)
    17→ * Related to: Feature 048-prevent-duplicate-vulnerabilities
    18→ *
    19→ * @property id Unique identifier
    20→ * @property asset Asset this vulnerability affects (required)
    21→ * @property vulnerabilityId CVE or vulnerability identifier (e.g., "CVE-2016-2183")
    22→ * @property cvssSeverity CVSS severity level (e.g., "High", "Critical", "Medium", "Low", "Informational")
    23→ * @property vulnerableProductVersions Affected product/version information
    24→ * @property daysOpen Text representation of days open (e.g., "58 days") - display only
    25→ * @property scanTimestamp Discovery date (calculated from daysOpen for accurate overdue calculation)
    26→ * @property importTimestamp When this data was imported (same for all vulns in batch, for "current" query)
    27→ * @property patchPublicationDate When the vendor published the patch/fix (optional, from CrowdStrike API)
    28→ * @property createdAt When the record was first created in the database (auto-generated)
    29→ */
    30→@Entity
    31→@Table(
    32→    name = "vulnerability",
    33→    indexes = [
    34→        Index(name = "idx_vulnerability_asset", columnList = "asset_id"),
    35→        Index(name = "idx_vulnerability_asset_scan", columnList = "asset_id,scan_timestamp"),
    36→        Index(name = "idx_vulnerability_severity", columnList = "cvss_severity"),
    37→        // Query optimization indexes (Feature: Database Structure Optimization)
    38→        Index(name = "idx_vulnerability_cve", columnList = "vulnerability_id"),                    // CVE ID lookups
    39→        Index(name = "idx_vulnerability_asset_cve", columnList = "asset_id,vulnerability_id"),     // Duplicate detection
    40→        Index(name = "idx_vulnerability_product", columnList = "vulnerable_product_versions"),     // Product filtering
    41→        Index(name = "idx_vulnerability_scan_timestamp", columnList = "scan_timestamp"),           // Temporal queries
    42→        Index(name = "idx_vulnerability_import_ts", columnList = "asset_id,import_timestamp"),      // "Current" queries
    43→        Index(name = "idx_vulnerability_sort_order", columnList = "import_timestamp DESC, vulnerability_id")  // ORDER BY optimization for paginated queries
    44→    ]
    45→)
    46→@Serdeable
    47→data class Vulnerability(
    48→    @Id
    49→    @GeneratedValue(strategy = GenerationType.IDENTITY)
    50→    var id: Long? = null,
    51→
    52→    @ManyToOne(fetch = FetchType.LAZY)
    53→    @JoinColumn(name = "asset_id", nullable = false)
    54→    @JsonIgnore // Prevent lazy loading errors during JSON serialization
    55→    @NotNull
    56→    var asset: Asset,
    57→
    58→    @Column(name = "vulnerability_id", length = 255)
    59→    var vulnerabilityId: String? = null,
    60→
    61→    @Column(name = "cvss_severity", length = 50)
    62→    var cvssSeverity: String? = null,
    63→
    64→    @Column(name = "vulnerable_product_versions", length = 512)
    65→    var vulnerableProductVersions: String? = null,
    66→
    67→    @Column(name = "days_open", length = 50)
    68→    var daysOpen: String? = null,
    69→
    70→    @Column(name = "scan_timestamp", nullable = false)
    71→    @NotNull
    72→    var scanTimestamp: LocalDateTime,
    73→
    74→    @Column(name = "patch_publication_date")
    75→    var patchPublicationDate: LocalDateTime? = null,
    76→
    77→    @Column(name = "import_timestamp")
    78→    var importTimestamp: LocalDateTime? = null,
    79→
    80→    @Column(name = "created_at", updatable = false)
    81→    var createdAt: LocalDateTime? = null
    82→) {
    83→    @PrePersist
    84→    fun onCreate() {
    85→        val now = LocalDateTime.now()
    86→        createdAt = now
    87→        // Default importTimestamp to createdAt if not explicitly set
    88→        if (importTimestamp == null) {
    89→            importTimestamp = now
    90→        }
    91→    }
    92→
    93→    override fun toString(): String {
    94→        return "Vulnerability(id=$id, vulnerabilityId='$vulnerabilityId', " +
    95→                "cvssSeverity='$cvssSeverity', scanTimestamp=$scanTimestamp)"
    96→    }
    97→
    98→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean {
    99→        if (this === other) return true
   100→        if (other !is Vulnerability) return false
   101→        return id != null && id == other.id
   102→    }
   103→
   104→    override fun hashCode(): Int {
   105→        return id?.hashCode() ?: 0
   106→    }
   107→}
   108→

    26→@Serdeable
    27→data class Asset(
    28→    @Id
    29→    @GeneratedValue
    30→    var id: Long? = null,
    31→
    32→    @Column(nullable = false)
    33→    @NotBlank
    34→    @Size(max = 255)
    35→    var name: String,
    36→
    37→    @Column(nullable = false)
    38→    @NotBlank
    39→    var type: String,
    40→
    41→    @Column
    42→    var ip: String? = null,
    43→
    44→    /**
    45→     * Numeric representation of IP address for efficient range queries
    46→     * Feature: 020-i-want-to (IP Address Mapping)
    47→     * Computed from ip field in @PrePersist and @PreUpdate
    48→     * Example: "192.168.1.100" -> 3232235876
    49→     */
    50→    @Column(name = "ip_numeric", nullable = true)
    51→    var ipNumeric: Long? = null,
    52→
    53→    @Column(nullable = false)
    54→    @NotBlank
    55→    @Size(max = 255)
    56→    var owner: String,
    57→
    58→    @Column(length = 1024)
    59→    var description: String? = null,
    60→
    61→    @Column(name = "created_at", updatable = false)
    62→    var createdAt: LocalDateTime? = null,
    63→
    64→    @Column(name = "updated_at")
    65→    var updatedAt: LocalDateTime? = null,
    66→
    67→    /**
    68→     * Timestamp when asset was last seen in a scan
    69→     * Updated when new ScanResult is created for this asset
    70→     * Related to: Feature 002-implement-a-parsing (Nmap Scan Import)
    71→     */
    72→    @Column(name = "last_seen")
    73→    var lastSeen: LocalDateTime? = null,
    74→
    75→    /**
    76→     * Comma-separated group names this asset belongs to
    77→     * Example: "SVR-MS-DMZ,Production"
    78→     * Related to: Feature 003-i-want-to (Vulnerability Management System)
    79→     */
    80→    @Column(name = "groups", length = 512)
    81→    var groups: String? = null,
    82→
    83→    /**
    84→     * Cloud service account ID
    85→     * Related to: Feature 003-i-want-to (Vulnerability Management System)
    86→     */
    87→    @Column(name = "cloud_account_id", length = 255)
    88→    var cloudAccountId: String? = null,
    89→
    90→    /**
    91→     * Cloud service instance ID
    92→     * Related to: Feature 003-i-want-to (Vulnerability Management System)
    93→     */
    94→    @Column(name = "cloud_instance_id", length = 255)
    95→    var cloudInstanceId: String? = null,
    96→
    97→    /**
    98→     * Active Directory domain this asset belongs to
    99→     * Example: "MS.HOME"

   101→     */

   110→    @Column(name = "os_version", length = 255)
   111→    var osVersion: String? = null,

   113→    /**

   120→    @Enumerated(EnumType.STRING)

   135→     * Workgroups should be accessed via service layer, not directly from API responses.

   137→    @JsonIgnore
   138→    @ManyToMany(fetch = FetchType.LAZY)
   139→    @JoinTable(
   140→        name = "asset_workgroups",
   141→        joinColumns = [JoinColumn(name = "asset_id")],
   142→        inverseJoinColumns = [JoinColumn(name = "workgroup_id")]
   143→    )
   144→    var workgroups: MutableSet<Workgroup> = mutableSetOf(),
   145→
   146→    /**
   147→     * Dual ownership tracking - manual creator
   148→     * Feature: 008-create-an-additional (Workgroup-Based Access Control)
   149→     * User who manually created this asset via UI

## Summary: VulnerabilityException Entity, Exception Types, and AWS Account Access

Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, here's a complete analysis of the VulnerabilityException entity, exception type matching logic, and AWS account access mechanisms:

---

### 1. VulnerabilityException Entity and ExceptionType Enum

**File**: `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/domain/VulnerabilityException.kt`

**ExceptionType Enum** (4 types):
- **IP**: Applies to all vulnerabilities on a specific IP address
- **PRODUCT**: Applies to all vulnerabilities matching a product name/version pattern OR CVE ID
- **ASSET**: Applies to all vulnerabilities on a specific asset (by asset ID)
- **CVE**: Applies to specific CVE IDs (comma-separated), optionally scoped to one asset

**Key Fields**:
- `exceptionType: ExceptionType` - The exception type
- `targetValue: String (max 512 chars)` - Target value:
  - IP type: exact IP address (e.g., "192.168.1.10")
  - PRODUCT type: product name/version pattern or CVE ID (e.g., "OpenSSH 7.4" or "CVE-2023-29326")
  - ASSET type: not used (assetId field used instead)
  - CVE type: comma-separated CVE IDs (e.g., "CVE-2023-29326,CVE-2024-1234")
- `assetId: Long?` - Asset ID for ASSET-type and CVE-type exceptions
- `expirationDate: LocalDateTime?` - Expiration date (required for new exceptions, Feature 021)
- `reason: String (max 1024 chars)` - Justification for exception
- `createdBy: String` - User who created the exception
- `createdAt, updatedAt: LocalDateTime` - Audit timestamps

**Active Status Check**:
```kotlin
fun isActive(): Boolean {
    return expirationDate == null || expirationDate!!.isAfter(LocalDateTime.now())
}
```

---

### 2. Exception Matching Logic

**File**: `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/domain/VulnerabilityException.kt`

**`matches()` Method**:
```kotlin
fun matches(vulnerability: Vulnerability, asset: Asset): Boolean {
    if (!isActive()) return false
    
    return when (exceptionType) {
        ExceptionType.IP -> asset.ip == targetValue
        ExceptionType.PRODUCT -> {
            // Match either CVE ID or product version string
            vulnerability.vulnerabilityId == targetValue ||
            vulnerability.vulnerableProductVersions?.contains(targetValue) == true
        }
        ExceptionType.ASSET -> assetId != null && assetId == asset.id
        ExceptionType.CVE -> {
            val cveIds = targetValue.split(",").map { it.trim() }
            val cveMatches = vulnerability.vulnerabilityId in cveIds
            if (assetId != null) {
                cveMatches && assetId == asset.id
            } else {
                cveMatches
            }
        }
    }
}
```

**Key Matching Rules**:
- IP exceptions: Asset IP must exactly match the exception targetValue
- PRODUCT exceptions: Either the CVE ID OR the product version string can match
- ASSET exceptions: Asset ID must match; ignores targetValue entirely
- CVE exceptions: CVE ID must be in the comma-separated list; if assetId set, asset must also match

---

### 3. VulnerabilityExceptionService

**File**: `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/service/VulnerabilityExceptionService.kt`

**Key Methods**:

1. **`getActiveExceptions()`**: Returns non-expired exceptions only
   ```kotlin
   fun getActiveExceptions(): List<VulnerabilityException> {
       val now = LocalDateTime.now()
       return vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.findByExpirationDateIsNullOrExpirationDateGreaterThan(now)
   }
   ```

2. **`isVulnerabilityExcepted()`**: Checks if vulnerability is covered by any active exception
   ```kotlin
   fun isVulnerabilityExcepted(vulnerability: Vulnerability, asset: Asset): Boolean {
       val activeExceptions = getActiveExceptions()
       return activeExceptions.any { exception -> exception.matches(vulnerability, asset) }
   }
   ```

3. **`calculateAffectedCount()`** - Feature 021 Phase 3: Counts affected vulnerabilities by exception type
   - **IP type**: Uses `countByAssetIp(targetValue)`
   - **PRODUCT type**: Uses `countByVulnerableProductVersionsContainingIgnoreCase(targetValue)`
   - **ASSET type**: Uses `countByAssetId(assetId)`
   - **CVE type**: 
     - If assetId set: Uses `countByVulnerabilityIdInAndAssetId(cveIds, assetId)`
     - Otherwise: Uses `countByVulnerabilityIdIn(cveIds)`

4. **`createException()`** - Validates exception and creates it
   - For ASSET type: Requires assetId and validates asset exists
   - For CVE type: Validates CVE ID format (CVE-YYYY-NNNNN) and supports comma-separated lists
   - Normalizes CVE targetValue by trimming spaces around commas

5. **`updateException()` and `deleteException()`**: Standard CRUD operations

6. **`getAllExceptions()`**: Returns paginated exceptions with optional affected count calculation

---

### 4. VulnerabilityExceptionRepository

**File**: `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/repository/VulnerabilityExceptionRepository.kt`

**Key Query Methods**:
- `findByExpirationDateIsNullOrExpirationDateGreaterThan(date: LocalDateTime)` - Active exceptions
- `findByExceptionType(type: ExceptionType)` - Filter by type
- `findByCreatedBy(username: String)` - Exceptions created by user
- `findByExceptionTypeAndAssetId(type, assetId)` - ASSET-type exceptions for scheduler
- `findByExceptionTypeAndTargetValue(type, targetValue)` - PRODUCT-type exceptions for scheduler

---

### 5. UserMapping Entity - AWS Account Access

**File**: `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/domain/UserMapping.kt`

**Purpose**: Maps users to AWS accounts, domains, and IP addresses for access control

**Key Fields**:
- `email: String` - User's email (REQUIRED, normalized to lowercase)
- `awsAccountId: String?` - AWS account ID (12 numeric digits, optional)
- `domain: String?` - Organizational domain (normalized to lowercase, optional)
- `ipAddress: String?` - Single IP or CIDR notation (optional)
- `ipRangeType: IpRangeType?` - Type: SINGLE, CIDR, or DASH_RANGE
- `ipRangeStart, ipRangeEnd: Long?` - Numeric IP range boundaries
- `user: User?` - Reference to User entity (nullable for future mappings, Feature 042)
- `appliedAt: Instant?` - When mapping was applied to user (Feature 042)
- `status: MappingStatus` - PENDING or ACTIVE (Feature 049)

**Business Rules**:
- At least one of: AWS Account ID, Domain, or IP Address must be provided
- Unique constraint on (email, awsAccountId, domain, ipAddress)
- Email and domain normalized to lowercase
- User reference nullable for future user mappings (Feature 042)
- Status automatically set to PENDING if user is null

**Helper Methods**:
```kotlin
fun isFutureMapping(): Boolean = user == null && appliedAt == null
fun isAppliedMapping(): Boolean = appliedAt != null
```

---

### 6. AwsAccountSharing Entity - Directional Sharing

**File**: `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/domain/AwsAccountSharing.kt`

**Purpose**: Directional, non-transitive sharing of AWS account visibility between users

**Key Fields**:
- `sourceUser: User` - User whose AWS accounts are being shared
- `targetUser: User` - User receiving visibility
- `createdBy: User` - Admin who created the sharing rule
- `createdAt, updatedAt: Instant` - Audit timestamps

**Semantics**:
- **Directional**: A→B does NOT imply B→A
- **Non-transitive**: A→B and B→C does NOT mean C sees A's accounts
- **Unique Constraint**: (sourceUserId, targetUserId) prevents duplicates
- **ADMIN-only**: Only ADMIN users can create/manage sharing rules

**Example**:
- sourceUser "alice@example.com" has AWS account "123456789012" in UserMapping
- awsAccountSharing created: sourceUser=alice, targetUser=bob
- Result: bob can now see assets with cloudAccountId="123456789012"

---

### 7. UserMappingRepository

**File**: `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/repository/UserMappingRepository.kt`

**Key Query Methods**:

**AWS Account Access**:
- `findDistinctAwsAccountIdByEmail(email: String)` - Get all AWS accounts user can access
- `countDistinctAwsAccountsByEmail(email: String)` - Count unique AWS accounts

**Domain Access**:
- `findDistinctDomainByEmail(email: String)` - Get all domains user can access
- `findDistinctDomains()` - Get all unique domains in system

**IP Address Access** (Feature 020):
- `findIpMappingsByEmail(email: String)` - Get all IP mappings for user
- `countIpMappingsByEmail(email: String)` - Count IP mappings

**Duplicate Detection**:
- `existsByEmailAndAwsAccountIdAndDomain()` - Check if mapping exists
- `findByEmailAndAwsAccountIdAndDomain()` - Get mapping by composite key

**Future User Mappings** (Feature 042):
- `findByAppliedAtIsNull()` - Current mappings (paginated)
- `findByAppliedAtIsNotNull()` - Applied historical mappings (paginated)
- `findByEmailIgnoreCase()` - Find mapping by email (case-insensitive)

**Status Filtering** (Feature 049):
- `findByEmailAndStatus()` - Find mappings by email and status (PENDING/ACTIVE)

---

### 8. AwsAccountSharingRepository

**File**: `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/repository/AwsAccountSharingRepository.kt`

**Key Query Methods**:

**Sharing Rules**:
- `findByTargetUserId(targetUserId: Long)` - Get all sharing rules for target user
- `findBySourceUserId(sourceUserId: Long)` - Get all sharing rules for source user
- `existsBySourceUserIdAndTargetUserId()` - Check if sharing exists
- `findBySourceUserIdAndTargetUserId()` - Get specific sharing rule

**Critical Access Control Query**:
```kotlin
@Query("""
    SELECT DISTINCT um.aws_account_id
    FROM aws_account_sharing acs
    JOIN users u_source ON u_source.id = acs.source_user_id
    JOIN user_mapping um ON um.email = u_source.email AND um.aws_account_id IS NOT NULL
    WHERE acs.target_user_id = :targetUserId
""", nativeQuery = true)
fun findSharedAwsAccountIdsByTargetUserId(targetUserId: Long): List<String>
```

This query resolves shared AWS accounts by:
1. Finding all sharing rules where targetUser is recipient
2. Looking up sourceUser's email from users table
3. Finding all UserMappings for that email with AWS account IDs
4. Returning distinct AWS account IDs

---

### 9. DTOs

**File**: `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/dto/VulnerabilityExceptionDto.kt`

**VulnerabilityExceptionDto**:
- Response DTO with all exception fields plus `isActive` flag
- Optional `affectedVulnerabilityCount` (Feature 021 Phase 3)
- Optional `assetId` and `assetName` (Feature 021)

**CreateVulnerabilityExceptionRequest**:
- Required: `exceptionType`, `targetValue`, `reason`
- Optional: `expirationDate` (but required for persistence)
- Optional: `assetId` (required for ASSET type, optional for others)

**UpdateVulnerabilityExceptionRequest**:
- Same fields as CreateVulnerabilityExceptionRequest

**OverdueStatus Enum** (Feature 021):
- `OK` - Within acceptable age threshold
- `OVERDUE` - Exceeds threshold with no active exception
- `EXCEPTED` - Has active exception covering vulnerability

**File**: `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/dto/VulnerabilityConfigDto.kt`

---

### 10. Related Enums

**File**: `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/domain/MappingStatus.kt`

```kotlin
enum class MappingStatus {
    PENDING,  // Created for non-existent user (future mapping)
    ACTIVE    // Applied to existing user
}
```

**File**: `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/domain/IpRangeType.kt`

```kotlin
enum class IpRangeType {
    SINGLE,      // Single IPv4 address (192.168.1.100)
    CIDR,        // CIDR notation (192.168.1.0/24)
    DASH_RANGE   // Dash range (192.168.1.1-192.168.1.100)
}
```

---

### 11. Unified Access Control (from CLAUDE.md)

Users access assets if **ANY** of these is true:
1. User has ADMIN role (universal)
2. Asset in user's workgroup
3. Asset manually created by user
4. Asset discovered via user's scan upload
5. Asset's cloudAccountId matches user's AWS mappings (UserMapping)
6. Asset's adDomain matches user's domain mappings (case-insensitive, UserMapping)
7. Asset's cloudAccountId matches shared AWS accounts via AwsAccountSharing (directional, non-transitive)
8. Asset's owner matches user's username

---

This comprehensive analysis covers all the exception types, matching logic, and AWS account access determination mechanisms in the secman system.

**File**: `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/dto/VulnerabilityExceptionDto.kt`

**VulnerabilityExceptionDto**:
- Response DTO with all exception fields plus `isActive` flag
- Optional `affectedVulnerabilityCount` (Feature 021 Phase 3)
- Optional `assetId` and `assetName` (Feature 021)

**CreateVulnerabilityExceptionRequest**:
- Required: `exceptionType`, `targetValue`, `reason`
- Optional: `expirationDate` (but required for persistence)
- Optional: `assetId` (required for ASSET type, optional for others)

**UpdateVulnerabilityExceptionRequest**:
- Same fields as CreateVulnerabilityExceptionRequest

**OverdueStatus Enum** (Feature 021):
- `OK` - Within acceptable age threshold
- `OVERDUE` - Exceeds threshold with no active exception
- `EXCEPTED` - Has active exception covering vulnerability

**File**: `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/dto/VulnerabilityConfigDto.kt`

### 10. Related Enums

```kotlin
    PENDING,  // Created for non-existent user (future mapping)
    ACTIVE    // Applied to existing user
}
```

```kotlin
enum class IpRangeType {
    SINGLE,      // Single IPv4 address (192.168.1.100)
    CIDR,        // CIDR notation (192.168.1.0/24)
    DASH_RANGE   // Dash range (192.168.1.1-192.168.1.100)
}
```

---

### 11. Unified Access Control (from CLAUDE.md)

Users access assets if **ANY** of these is true:
1. User has ADMIN role (universal)
2. Asset in user's workgroup
3. Asset manually created by user
4. Asset discovered via user's scan upload
5. Asset's cloudAccountId matches user's AWS mappings (UserMapping)
6. Asset's adDomain matches user's domain mappings (case-insensitive, UserMapping)
7. Asset's cloudAccountId matches shared AWS accounts via AwsAccountSharing (directional, non-transitive)
8. Asset's owner matches user's username

    24→
    25→/**
    26→ * REST API controller for vulnerability exception request workflow.
    27→ *
    28→ * Endpoints:
    29→ * - POST /api/vulnerability-exception-requests - Create new request
    30→ * - GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/my - Get user's own requests
    31→ * - GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/my/summary - Get user's request statistics
    32→ * - GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id} - Get request by ID
    33→ * - POST /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id}/approve - Approve request (ADMIN/SECCHAMPION only)
    34→ * - POST /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id}/reject - Reject request (ADMIN/SECCHAMPION only)
    35→ * - DELETE /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id} - Cancel own pending request
    36→ * - GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/pending - Get all pending requests (ADMIN/SECCHAMPION only)
    37→ * - GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/pending/count - Get pending count for badge
    38→ *
    39→ * Concurrency Control: First-approver-wins using optimistic locking
    40→ * Audit Logging: All state transitions logged asynchronously
    41→ *
    42→ * Feature: 031-vuln-exception-approval
    43→ * Reference: contracts/exception-request-api.yaml, quickstart.md
    44→ */
    45→@Controller("/api/vulnerability-exception-requests")
    46→@ExecuteOn(TaskExecutors.BLOCKING)
    47→open class VulnerabilityExceptionRequestController(
    48→    private val exceptionRequestService: VulnerabilityExceptionRequestService,
    49→    private val statisticsService: ExceptionRequestStatisticsService,
    50→    private val exportService: ExceptionRequestExportService
    51→) {
    52→    private val logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(VulnerabilityExceptionRequestController::class.java)
    53→
    54→    @Serdeable
    55→    data class ErrorResponse(
    56→        val error: String,
    57→        val details: String? = null,
    58→        val status: Int
    59→    )
    60→
    61→    @Serdeable
    62→    data class PendingCountResponse(val count: Long)
    63→
    64→    /**
    65→     * TASK-016: Create a new exception request.
    66→     *
    67→     * POST /api/vulnerability-exception-requests
    68→     * Auth: All authenticated users
    69→     * Auto-approval: If user has ADMIN or SECCHAMPION role, status = APPROVED immediately
    70→     *
    71→     * @param dto Request creation data (vulnerabilityId, scope, reason, expirationDate)
    72→     * @param authentication Current authenticated user
    73→     * @param request HTTP request for IP address extraction
    74→     * @return 201 Created with request DTO, 400 if validation fails, 409 if duplicate active request
    75→     */
    76→    @Post
    77→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_AUTHENTICATED)
    78→    open fun createRequest(
    79→        @Valid @Body dto: CreateExceptionRequestDto,
    80→        authentication: Authentication,
    81→        request: HttpRequest<*>
    82→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
    83→        return try {
    84→            val userId = getUserId(authentication)
    85→            val clientIp = request.remoteAddress.address.hostAddress
    86→
    87→            logger.debug(
    88→                "Creating exception request: userId={}, vulnerabilityId={}, scope={}",
    89→                userId, dto.vulnerabilityId, dto.scope
    90→            )
    91→
    92→            val createdRequest = exceptionRequestService.createRequest(dto, userId, clientIp)
    93→
    94→            logger.info(
    95→                "Exception request created: requestId={}, status={}, autoApproved={}",
    96→                createdRequest.id, createdRequest.status, createdRequest.autoApproved
    97→            )
    98→
    99→            HttpResponse.created(createdRequest)
   100→
   101→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
   102→            logger.warn("Invalid request: {}", e.message)
   103→            HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse(
   104→                error = e.message ?: "Invalid request",
   105→                status = 400
   106→            ))
   107→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   108→            logger.error("Failed to create exception request", e)
   109→            HttpResponse.serverError<ErrorResponse>()
   110→        }
   111→    }
   112→
   113→    /**
   114→     * TASK-017: Get user's own exception requests with optional filtering and pagination.
   115→     *
   116→     * GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/my?status=PENDING&page=0&size=20
   117→     * Auth: All authenticated users
   118→     *
   119→     * @param authentication Current authenticated user
   120→     * @param status Optional status filter (PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, EXPIRED, CANCELLED)
   121→     * @param page Page number (0-indexed, default: 0)
   122→     * @param size Page size (default: 20, options: 20/50/100)
   123→     * @return 200 OK with paginated requests
   124→     */
   125→    @Get("/my")
   126→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_AUTHENTICATED)
   127→    open fun getUserRequests(
   128→        authentication: Authentication,
   129→        @Nullable @QueryValue status: ExceptionRequestStatus?,
   130→        @Nullable @QueryValue page: Int?,
   131→        @Nullable @QueryValue size: Int?
   132→    ): HttpResponse<PaginatedExceptionRequestsResponse> {
   133→        return try {
   134→            val userId = getUserId(authentication)
   135→            val pageNumber = maxOf(page ?: 0, 0)
   136→            val pageSize = when (size) {
   137→                20, 50, 100 -> size
   138→                else -> 20 // Default to 20
   139→            }
   140→
   141→            logger.debug(
   142→                "Getting user requests: userId={}, status={}, page={}, size={}",
   143→                userId, status, pageNumber, pageSize
   144→            )
   145→
   146→            val pageable = Pageable.from(pageNumber, pageSize, Sort.of(Sort.Order.desc("createdAt")))
   147→            val requests = exceptionRequestService.getUserRequests(userId, status, pageable)
   148→
   149→            val wrapped = PaginatedExceptionRequestsResponse(
   150→                content = requests.content,
   151→                totalElements = requests.totalSize,
   152→                totalPages = requests.totalPages
   153→            )
   154→            HttpResponse.ok(wrapped)
   155→
   156→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   157→            logger.error("Failed to get user requests", e)
   158→            HttpResponse.serverError<PaginatedExceptionRequestsResponse>()
   159→        }
   160→    }
   161→
   162→    /**
   163→     * TASK-018: Get summary statistics for user's exception requests.
   164→     *
   165→     * GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/my/summary
   166→     * Auth: All authenticated users
   167→     *
   168→     * @param authentication Current authenticated user
   169→     * @return 200 OK with summary statistics (total, approved, pending, rejected, expired, cancelled)
   170→     */
   171→    @Get("/my/summary")
   172→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_AUTHENTICATED)
   173→    open fun getUserRequestSummary(
   174→        authentication: Authentication
   175→    ): HttpResponse<ExceptionRequestSummaryDto> {
   176→        return try {
   177→            val userId = getUserId(authentication)
   178→
   179→            logger.debug("Getting user request summary: userId={}", userId)
   180→
   181→            val summary = exceptionRequestService.getUserRequestSummary(userId)
   182→
   183→            HttpResponse.ok(summary)
   184→
   185→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   186→            logger.error("Failed to get user request summary", e)
   187→            HttpResponse.serverError<ExceptionRequestSummaryDto>()
   188→        }
   189→    }
   190→
   191→    /**
   192→     * TASK-019: Get exception request by ID.
   193→     *
   194→     * GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id}
   195→     * Auth: All authenticated users
   196→     * Access control: Users can only view own requests, ADMIN/SECCHAMPION can view all
   197→     *
   198→     * @param id Request ID
   199→     * @param authentication Current authenticated user
   200→     * @return 200 OK with request DTO, 403 if not owner (unless ADMIN/SECCHAMPION), 404 if not found
   201→     */
   202→    @Get("/{id}")
   203→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_AUTHENTICATED)
   204→    open fun getRequestById(
   205→        @PathVariable id: Long,
   206→        authentication: Authentication
   207→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
   208→        return try {
   209→            val userId = getUserId(authentication)
   210→            val request = exceptionRequestService.getRequestById(id)
   211→
   212→            // Access control: User must own request OR have ADMIN/SECCHAMPION role
   213→            val isOwner = request.requestedByUsername == authentication.name
   214→            val isAdminOrSecChampion = hasRole(authentication, "ADMIN") || hasRole(authentication, "SECCHAMPION")
   215→
   216→            if (!isOwner && !isAdminOrSecChampion) {
   217→                logger.warn("Access denied: userId={} attempted to view request {}", userId, id)
   218→                return HttpResponse.status<ErrorResponse>(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN).body(
   219→                    ErrorResponse(
   220→                        error = "Access denied",
   221→                        details = "You can only view your own requests",
   222→                        status = 403
   223→                    )
   224→                )
   225→            }
   226→
   227→            HttpResponse.ok(request)
   228→
   229→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
   230→            logger.warn("Request not found: id={}", id)
   231→            HttpResponse.notFound<VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto>()
   232→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   233→            logger.error("Failed to get request by ID: id={}", id, e)
   234→            HttpResponse.serverError<VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto>()
   235→        }
   236→    }
   237→
   238→    /**
   239→     * TASK-020: Approve a pending exception request.
   240→     *
   241→     * POST /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id}/approve
   242→     * Auth: ADMIN or SECCHAMPION only
   243→     * Concurrency: First-approver-wins (optimistic locking)
   244→     *
   245→     * @param id Request ID
   246→     * @param reviewDto Optional review comment
   247→     * @param authentication Current authenticated user (reviewer)
   248→     * @param request HTTP request for IP address extraction
   249→     * @return 200 OK with updated request, 404 if not found, 409 if concurrent approval
   250→     */
   251→    @Post("/{id}/approve")
   252→    @Secured("ADMIN", "SECCHAMPION")
   253→    open fun approveRequest(
   254→        @PathVariable id: Long,
   255→        @Nullable @Body reviewDto: ReviewExceptionRequestDto?,
   256→        authentication: Authentication,
   257→        request: HttpRequest<*>
   258→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
   259→        return try {
   260→            val reviewerId = getUserId(authentication)
   261→            val clientIp = request.remoteAddress.address.hostAddress
   262→
   263→            logger.debug("Approving request: requestId={}, reviewerId={}", id, reviewerId)
   264→
   265→            val approvedRequest = exceptionRequestService.approveRequest(
   266→                requestId = id,
   267→                reviewerUserId = reviewerId,
   268→                reviewDto = reviewDto,
   269→                clientIp = clientIp
   270→            )
   271→
   272→            logger.info("Request approved: requestId={}, reviewerId={}", id, reviewerId)
   273→
   274→            HttpResponse.ok(approvedRequest)
   275→
   276→        } catch (e: ConcurrentApprovalException) {
   277→            // TASK-025: Handle concurrent approval conflicts
   278→            logger.warn("Concurrent approval conflict: requestId={}, {}", id, e.getDetailedMessage())
   279→            HttpResponse.status<ErrorResponse>(HttpStatus.CONFLICT).body(
   280→                ErrorResponse(
   281→                    error = e.getUserMessage(),
   282→                    details = e.getDetailedMessage(),
   283→                    status = 409
   284→                )
   285→            )
   286→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
   287→            logger.warn("Invalid approval request: requestId={}, error={}", id, e.message)
   288→            HttpResponse.notFound<VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto>()
   289→        } catch (e: IllegalStateException) {
   290→            // TASK-026: Handle invalid status transitions
   291→            logger.warn("Invalid status transition: requestId={}, error={}", id, e.message)
   292→            HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse(
   293→                error = "Invalid status transition",
   294→                details = e.message,
   295→                status = 400
   296→            ))
   297→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   298→            logger.error("Failed to approve request: requestId={}", id, e)
   299→            HttpResponse.serverError<VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto>()
   300→        }
   301→    }
   302→
   303→    /**
   304→     * TASK-021: Reject a pending exception request.
   305→     *
   306→     * POST /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id}/reject
   307→     * Auth: ADMIN or SECCHAMPION only
   308→     * Required: Review comment (minimum 10 characters)
   309→     *
   310→     * @param id Request ID
   311→     * @param reviewDto Review comment (required, 10-1024 characters)
   312→     * @param authentication Current authenticated user (reviewer)
   313→     * @param request HTTP request for IP address extraction
   314→     * @return 200 OK with updated request, 400 if comment missing, 404 if not found, 409 if concurrent rejection
   315→     */
   316→    @Post("/{id}/reject")
   317→    @Secured("ADMIN", "SECCHAMPION")
   318→    open fun rejectRequest(
   319→        @PathVariable id: Long,
   320→        @Valid @Body reviewDto: ReviewExceptionRequestDto,
   321→        authentication: Authentication,
   322→        request: HttpRequest<*>
   323→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
   324→        return try {
   325→            val reviewerId = getUserId(authentication)
   326→            val clientIp = request.remoteAddress.address.hostAddress
   327→
   328→            logger.debug("Rejecting request: requestId={}, reviewerId={}", id, reviewerId)
   329→
   330→            val rejectedRequest = exceptionRequestService.rejectRequest(
   331→                requestId = id,
   332→                reviewerUserId = reviewerId,
   333→                reviewDto = reviewDto,
   334→                clientIp = clientIp
   335→            )
   336→
   337→            logger.info("Request rejected: requestId={}, reviewerId={}", id, reviewerId)
   338→
   339→            HttpResponse.ok(rejectedRequest)
   340→
   341→        } catch (e: ConcurrentApprovalException) {
   342→            // TASK-025: Handle concurrent review conflicts
   343→            logger.warn("Concurrent rejection conflict: requestId={}, {}", id, e.getDetailedMessage())
   344→            HttpResponse.status<ErrorResponse>(HttpStatus.CONFLICT).body(
   345→                ErrorResponse(
   346→                    error = e.getUserMessage(),
   347→                    details = e.getDetailedMessage(),
   348→                    status = 409
   349→                )
   350→            )
   351→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
   352→            logger.warn("Invalid rejection request: requestId={}, error={}", id, e.message)
   353→            HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse(
   354→                error = "Validation failed",
   355→                details = e.message,
   356→                status = 400
   357→            ))
   358→        } catch (e: IllegalStateException) {
   359→            // TASK-026: Handle invalid status transitions
   360→            logger.warn("Invalid status transition: requestId={}, error={}", id, e.message)
   361→            HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse(
   362→                error = "Invalid status transition",
   363→                details = e.message,
   364→                status = 400
   365→            ))
   366→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   367→            logger.error("Failed to reject request: requestId={}", id, e)
   368→            HttpResponse.serverError<VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto>()
   369→        }
   370→    }
   371→
   372→    /**
   373→     * TASK-022: Cancel own pending exception request.
   374→     *
   375→     * DELETE /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id}
   376→     * Auth: All authenticated users
   377→     * Access control: Only requester can cancel, only PENDING status can be cancelled
   378→     *
   379→     * @param id Request ID
   380→     * @param authentication Current authenticated user
   381→     * @param request HTTP request for IP address extraction
   382→     * @return 204 No Content, 403 if not owner, 400 if not PENDING, 404 if not found
   383→     */
   384→    @Delete("/{id}")
   385→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_AUTHENTICATED)
   386→    @Status(HttpStatus.NO_CONTENT)
   387→    open fun cancelRequest(
   388→        @PathVariable id: Long,
   389→        authentication: Authentication,
   390→        request: HttpRequest<*>
   391→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
   392→        return try {
   393→            val userId = getUserId(authentication)
   394→            val clientIp = request.remoteAddress.address.hostAddress
   395→
   396→            logger.debug("Cancelling request: requestId={}, userId={}", id, userId)
   397→
   398→            exceptionRequestService.cancelRequest(
   399→                requestId = id,
   400→                requesterUserId = userId,
   401→                clientIp = clientIp
   402→            )
   403→
   404→            logger.info("Request cancelled: requestId={}, userId={}", id, userId)
   405→
   406→            HttpResponse.noContent<Void>()
   407→
   408→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
   409→            logger.warn("Cannot cancel request: requestId={}, userId={}, error={}", id, getUserId(authentication), e.message)
   410→            if (e.message?.contains("original requester") == true) {
   411→                HttpResponse.status<ErrorResponse>(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN).body(
   412→                    ErrorResponse(
   413→                        error = "Access denied",
   414→                        details = "Only the original requester can cancel this request",
   415→                        status = 403
   416→                    )
   417→                )
   418→            } else {
   419→                HttpResponse.notFound<ErrorResponse>()
   420→            }
   421→        } catch (e: IllegalStateException) {
   422→            // TASK-026: Handle invalid status transitions
   423→            logger.warn("Invalid cancellation: requestId={}, error={}", id, e.message)
   424→            HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse(
   425→                error = "Cannot cancel this request",
   426→                details = e.message,
   427→                status = 400
   428→            ))
   429→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   430→            logger.error("Failed to cancel request: requestId={}", id, e)
   431→            HttpResponse.serverError<ErrorResponse>()
   432→        }
   433→    }
   434→
   435→    /**
   436→     * TASK-023: Get all pending exception requests (for approval dashboard).
   437→     *
   438→     * GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/pending?page=0&size=20
   439→     * Auth: ADMIN or SECCHAMPION only
   440→     * Sorted by: created_at ASC (oldest first)
   441→     *
   442→     * @param authentication Current authenticated user
   443→     * @param page Page number (0-indexed, default: 0)
   444→     * @param size Page size (default: 20, options: 20/50/100)
   445→     * @return 200 OK with paginated pending requests
   446→     */
   447→    @Get("/pending")
   448→    @Secured("ADMIN", "SECCHAMPION")
   449→    open fun getPendingRequests(
   450→        authentication: Authentication,
   451→        @Nullable @QueryValue page: Int?,
   452→        @Nullable @QueryValue size: Int?
   453→    ): HttpResponse<PaginatedExceptionRequestsResponse> {
   454→        return try {
   455→            val pageNumber = maxOf(page ?: 0, 0)
   456→            val pageSize = when (size) {
   457→                20, 50, 100 -> size
   458→                else -> 20 // Default to 20
   459→            }
   460→
   461→            logger.debug("Getting pending requests: page={}, size={}", pageNumber, pageSize)
   462→
   463→            // Sort by created_at ASC (oldest first) per FR-020
   464→            val pageable = Pageable.from(pageNumber, pageSize, Sort.of(Sort.Order.asc("createdAt")))
   465→            val pendingRequests = exceptionRequestService.getPendingRequests(pageable)
   466→
   467→            logger.info("Returning {} pending requests (page {}, total {})",
   468→                pendingRequests.content.size, pageNumber, pendingRequests.totalSize)
   469→
   470→            val wrapped = PaginatedExceptionRequestsResponse(
   471→                content = pendingRequests.content,
   472→                totalElements = pendingRequests.totalSize,
   473→                totalPages = pendingRequests.totalPages
   474→            )
   475→            HttpResponse.ok(wrapped)
   476→
   477→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   478→            logger.error("Failed to get pending requests", e)
   479→            HttpResponse.serverError<PaginatedExceptionRequestsResponse>()
   480→        }
   481→    }
   482→
   483→    /**
   484→     * TASK-024: Get count of pending exception requests (for badge display).
   485→     *
   486→     * GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/pending/count
   487→     * Auth: ADMIN or SECCHAMPION only
   488→     * Used for: Real-time badge updates (SSE will call this periodically)
   489→     *
   490→     * @param authentication Current authenticated user
   491→     * @return 200 OK with { "count": number }
   492→     */
   493→    @Get("/pending/count")
   494→    @Secured("ADMIN", "SECCHAMPION")
   495→    open fun getPendingCount(
   496→        authentication: Authentication
   497→    ): HttpResponse<PendingCountResponse> {
   498→        return try {
   499→            val count = exceptionRequestService.getPendingCount()
   500→
   501→            logger.debug("Pending count requested: count={}", count)
   502→
   503→            HttpResponse.ok(PendingCountResponse(count = count))
   504→
   505→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   506→            logger.error("Failed to get pending count", e)

   510→
   511→    /**
   512→     * GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/statistics
   513→     *
   514→     * Get statistical metrics for exception requests.
   515→     *
   516→     * **Access Control**: ADMIN and SECCHAMPION roles only
   517→     *
   518→     * **Query Parameters**:
   519→     * - dateRange: Optional (7days, 30days, 90days, alltime), default 30days
   520→     *
   521→     * **Response**: 200 OK with ExceptionStatisticsDto containing:
   522→     * - totalRequests: Total count in date range
   523→     * - approvalRatePercent: Approval percentage (null if no reviewed requests)
   524→     * - averageApprovalTimeHours: Median approval time in hours (null if no approved requests)
   525→     * - requestsByStatus: Map of status → count
   526→     * - topRequesters: Top 10 requesters with counts
   527→     * - topCVEs: Top 10 CVEs with counts
   528→     *
   529→     * Feature: 031-vuln-exception-approval
   530→     * User Story 8: Analytics & Reporting (P3)
   531→     * Phase 11: Analytics & Reporting
   532→     * Reference: spec.md acceptance scenario US8-1
   533→     */
   534→    @Get("/statistics")
   535→    @Secured("ADMIN", "SECCHAMPION")
   536→    open fun getStatistics(
   537→        authentication: Authentication,
   538→        @QueryValue @Nullable dateRange: String?
   539→    ): HttpResponse<ExceptionStatisticsDto> {
   540→        return try {
   541→            val effectiveDateRange = dateRange ?: "30days"
   542→
   543→            logger.info("Statistics requested: dateRange={}, user={}", effectiveDateRange, authentication.name)
   544→
   545→            // Get metrics from statistics service
   546→            val approvalRate = statisticsService.getApprovalRate(effectiveDateRange)
   547→            val averageApprovalTime = statisticsService.getAverageApprovalTime(effectiveDateRange)
   548→            val requestsByStatus = statisticsService.getRequestsByStatus(effectiveDateRange)
   549→            val topRequesters = statisticsService.getTopRequesters(10, effectiveDateRange)
   550→            val topCVEs = statisticsService.getTopCVEs(10, effectiveDateRange)
   551→
   552→            // Calculate total requests
   553→            val totalRequests = requestsByStatus.values.sum()
   554→
   555→            // Convert status map to string keys for JSON serialization
   556→            val statusMap = requestsByStatus.mapKeys { it.key.name }
   557→
   558→            // Convert to DTOs
   559→            val topRequesterDtos = topRequesters.map { (username, count) ->
   560→                TopRequesterDto(username, count)
   561→            }
   562→            val topCVEDtos = topCVEs.map { (cveId, count) ->
   563→                TopCVEDto(cveId, count)
   564→            }
   565→
   566→            val statistics = ExceptionStatisticsDto(
   567→                totalRequests = totalRequests,
   568→                approvalRatePercent = approvalRate,
   569→                averageApprovalTimeHours = averageApprovalTime,
   570→                requestsByStatus = statusMap,
   571→                topRequesters = topRequesterDtos,
   572→                topCVEs = topCVEDtos
   573→            )
   574→
   575→            logger.debug("Statistics computed: totalRequests={}, approvalRate={}, avgTime={}h",
   576→                totalRequests, approvalRate, averageApprovalTime)
   577→
   578→            HttpResponse.ok(statistics)
   579→
   580→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   581→            logger.error("Failed to get statistics: dateRange={}", dateRange, e)
   582→            HttpResponse.serverError<ExceptionStatisticsDto>()
   583→        }
   584→    }
   585→
   586→    /**
   587→     * GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/export
   588→     *
   589→     * Export exception requests to Excel with optional filtering.
   590→     *
   591→     * **Access Control**: ADMIN and SECCHAMPION roles only
   592→     *
   593→     * **Query Parameters**:
   594→     * - status: Optional status filter (PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, EXPIRED, CANCELLED)
   595→     * - dateRange: Optional (7days, 30days, 90days, alltime)
   596→     * - requesterId: Optional requester user ID filter
   597→     * - reviewerId: Optional reviewer user ID filter
   598→     *
   599→     * **Response**: 200 OK with Excel file
   600→     * - Content-Type: application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet
   601→     * - Filename: exception-requests-{timestamp}.xlsx
   602→     * - Columns: Request ID, CVE ID, Asset Name, Asset IP, Requester, Submission Date,
   603→     *   Status, Reviewer, Review Date, Reason, Review Comment, Expiration Date, Auto-Approved
   604→     *
   605→     * Feature: 031-vuln-exception-approval
   606→     * User Story 8: Analytics & Reporting (P3)
   607→     * Phase 11: Analytics & Reporting
   608→     * Reference: spec.md FR-026, acceptance scenario US8-3
   609→     */
   610→    @Get("/export")
   611→    @Secured("ADMIN", "SECCHAMPION")
   612→    @Produces("application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet")
   613→    open fun exportToExcel(
   614→        authentication: Authentication,
   615→        @QueryValue @Nullable status: String?,
   616→        @QueryValue @Nullable dateRange: String?,
   617→        @QueryValue @Nullable requesterId: Long?,
   618→        @QueryValue @Nullable reviewerId: Long?
   619→    ): HttpResponse<ByteArray> {
   620→        return try {
   621→            logger.info("Excel export requested: status={}, dateRange={}, requesterId={}, reviewerId={}, user={}",
   622→                status, dateRange, requesterId, reviewerId, authentication.name)
   623→
   624→            // Parse status if provided
   625→            val statusEnum = status?.let {
   626→                try {
   627→                    ExceptionRequestStatus.valueOf(it.uppercase())
   628→                } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
   629→                    logger.warn("Invalid status parameter: {}", it)
   630→                    null
   631→                }
   632→            }
   633→
   634→            // Generate Excel export
   635→            val excelData = exportService.exportToExcel(
   636→                status = statusEnum,
   637→                dateRange = dateRange,
   638→                requesterId = requesterId,
   639→                reviewerId = reviewerId
   640→            )
   641→
   642→            // Generate filename with timestamp
   643→            val timestamp = java.time.LocalDateTime.now().format(
   644→                java.time.format.DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("yyyyMMdd-HHmmss")
   645→            )
   646→            val filename = "exception-requests-$timestamp.xlsx"
   647→
   648→            logger.info("Excel export completed: filename={}, size={} bytes", filename, excelData.size())
   649→
   650→            HttpResponse.ok(excelData.toByteArray())
   651→                .header("Content-Disposition", "attachment; filename=\"$filename\"")
   652→
   653→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   654→            logger.error("Failed to export Excel: status={}, dateRange={}", status, dateRange, e)
   655→            HttpResponse.serverError<ByteArray>()
   656→        }
   657→    }
   658→
   659→    /**
   660→     * Extract user ID from authentication object.
   661→     *
   662→     * @param authentication Current authenticated user
   663→     * @return User ID as Long
   664→     * @throws IllegalStateException if user ID not found in attributes
   665→     */
   666→    private fun getUserId(authentication: Authentication): Long {
   667→        return authentication.attributes["userId"]?.toString()?.toLong()
   668→            ?: throw IllegalStateException("User ID not found in authentication context")
   669→    }
   670→
   671→    /**
   672→     * Check if authentication has a specific role.
   673→     *
   674→     * @param authentication Current authenticated user
   675→     * @param role Role name to check
   676→     * @return True if user has the role
   677→     */
   678→    private fun hasRole(authentication: Authentication, role: String): Boolean {
   679→        return authentication.roles.contains(role)
   680→    }
   681→}

1→package com.secman.dto
     2→
     3→import com.secman.domain.ExceptionScope
     4→import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable
     5→import jakarta.validation.constraints.Future
