    10→
    11→/**
    12→ * DTO for creating a new vulnerability exception request.
    13→ *
    14→ * Used for POST /api/vulnerability-exception-requests endpoint.
    15→ *
    16→ * Validation rules:
    17→ * - vulnerabilityId: Required, must reference existing vulnerability
    18→ * - scope: Required, SINGLE_VULNERABILITY or CVE_PATTERN
    19→ * - reason: Required, 50-2048 characters, business justification
    20→ * - expirationDate: Required, must be future date
    21→ *
    22→ * Feature: 031-vuln-exception-approval
    23→ * Reference: contracts/exception-request-api.yaml lines 73-92
    24→ */
    25→@Serdeable
    26→data class CreateExceptionRequestDto(
    27→    /**
    28→     * ID of the vulnerability to create exception for
    29→     */
    30→    @field:NotNull(message = "Vulnerability ID is required")
    31→    val vulnerabilityId: Long,
    32→
    33→    /**
    34→     * Exception scope: SINGLE_VULNERABILITY or CVE_PATTERN
    35→     * - SINGLE_VULNERABILITY: Applies only to this specific vulnerability on this specific asset
    36→     * - CVE_PATTERN: Applies to all vulnerabilities with this CVE across all assets
    37→     */
    38→    @field:NotNull(message = "Exception scope is required")
    39→    val scope: ExceptionScope,
    40→
    41→    /**
    42→     * Business justification for the exception
    43→     * Minimum 50 characters to ensure meaningful justification
    44→     * Maximum 2048 characters for database storage
    45→     */
    46→    @field:NotBlank(message = "Reason is required")
    47→    @field:Size(min = 50, max = 2048, message = "Reason must be between 50 and 2048 characters")
    48→    val reason: String,
    49→
    50→    /**
    51→     * When the exception should expire
    52→     * Must be a future date at time of creation
    53→     *
    54→     * Note: Validation uses @Future which checks against current date/time
    55→     * Users will receive warning if expiration > 365 days in future (client-side)
    56→     */
    57→    @field:NotNull(message = "Expiration date is required")
    58→    @field:Future(message = "Expiration date must be in the future")
    59→    val expirationDate: LocalDateTime
    60→)

     8→/**
     9→ * DTO for vulnerability exception request responses.

    12→ * - GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id}
    13→ * - GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/my
    14→ * - GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/pending
    15→ *
    16→ * Contains all request details including embedded vulnerability information
    17→ * for display without additional queries.
    18→ *
    19→ * Feature: 031-vuln-exception-approval
    20→ * Reference: contracts/exception-request-api.yaml lines 575-625
    21→ */
    22→@Serdeable
    23→data class VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto(
    24→    /**
    25→     * Unique identifier of the exception request
    26→     */
    27→    val id: Long,
    28→
    29→    /**
    30→     * ID of the vulnerability this request applies to
    31→     */
    32→    val vulnerabilityId: Long?,
    33→
    34→    /**
    35→     * CVE ID of the vulnerability (denormalized for display)
    36→     */
    37→    val vulnerabilityCve: String?,

    39→    /**
    40→     * Name of the asset with the vulnerability (denormalized for display)

    45→     * IP address of the asset (denormalized for display)

    51→     */

    53→
    54→    /**
    55→     * Exception scope: SINGLE_VULNERABILITY or CVE_PATTERN
    56→     */
    57→    val scope: ExceptionScope,
    58→
    59→    /**
    60→     * Business justification for the exception (50-2048 characters)
    61→     */
    62→    val reason: String,
    63→
    64→    /**
    65→     * When the exception should expire
    66→     */
    67→    val expirationDate: LocalDateTime,
    68→
    69→    /**
    70→     * Current status: PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, EXPIRED, CANCELLED
    71→     */
    72→    val status: ExceptionRequestStatus,
    73→
    74→    /**
    75→     * True if this request was auto-approved (requester has ADMIN/SECCHAMPION role)
    76→     */
    77→    val autoApproved: Boolean,
    78→
    79→    /**
    80→     * Username of the reviewer who approved/rejected
    81→     * Null if not yet reviewed
    82→     */
    83→    val reviewedByUsername: String?,
    84→
    85→    /**
    86→     * When the request was reviewed (approved or rejected)
    87→     * Null if not yet reviewed
    88→     */
    89→    val reviewDate: LocalDateTime?,
    90→
    91→    /**
    92→     * Reviewer's comment/justification
    93→     * Optional for approval, required for rejection
    94→     * Null if not reviewed or no comment provided
    95→     */
    96→    val reviewComment: String?,
    97→
    98→    /**
    99→     * When the request was created
   100→     */
   101→    val createdAt: LocalDateTime,
   102→
   103→    /**
   104→     * When the request was last updated
   105→     */
   106→    val updatedAt: LocalDateTime
   107→)
   108→
   109→@Serdeable
   110→data class PaginatedExceptionRequestsResponse(
   111→    val content: List<VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto> = emptyList(),
   112→    val totalElements: Long,
   113→    val totalPages: Int
   114→)
   115→

1→package com.secman.service
     2→
     3→import com.secman.domain.Asset
     4→import com.secman.domain.Vulnerability
     5→import com.secman.domain.VulnerabilityException
     6→import com.secman.domain.VulnerabilityException.ExceptionType
     7→import com.secman.dto.CreateVulnerabilityExceptionRequest
     8→import com.secman.dto.UpdateVulnerabilityExceptionRequest
     9→import com.secman.dto.VulnerabilityExceptionDto
    10→import com.secman.repository.AssetRepository
    11→import com.secman.repository.VulnerabilityExceptionRepository
    12→import com.secman.repository.VulnerabilityRepository
    13→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
    14→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    15→import java.time.LocalDateTime
    16→
    17→/**
    18→ * Service for vulnerability exception management
    19→ *
    20→ * Handles:
    21→ * - Creating, updating, deleting exceptions (IP, PRODUCT, ASSET, CVE types)
    22→ * - Checking if vulnerabilities are excepted
    23→ * - Managing exception lifecycle (active/expired)
    24→ *
    25→ * Related to: Feature 004-i-want-to (VULN Role & Vulnerability Management UI)
    26→ * Related to: Feature 021-vulnerability-overdue-exception-logic (Added ASSET support)
    27→ */
    28→@Singleton
    29→class VulnerabilityExceptionService(
    30→    private val vulnerabilityExceptionRepository: VulnerabilityExceptionRepository,
    31→    private val assetRepository: AssetRepository,
    32→    private val vulnerabilityRepository: VulnerabilityRepository  // Feature 021 Phase 3: For affected counts
    33→) {
    34→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(VulnerabilityExceptionService::class.java)
    35→
    36→    /**
    37→     * Get all active exceptions (not expired)
    38→     *
    39→     * @return List of active vulnerability exceptions
    40→     */
    41→    fun getActiveExceptions(): List<VulnerabilityException> {
    42→        val now = LocalDateTime.now()
    43→        return vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.findByExpirationDateIsNullOrExpirationDateGreaterThan(now)
    44→    }
    45→
    46→    /**
    47→     * Check if a vulnerability is excepted
    48→     *
    49→     * @param vulnerability The vulnerability to check
    50→     * @param asset The asset the vulnerability is on
    51→     * @return True if vulnerability is excepted, false otherwise
    52→     */
    53→    fun isVulnerabilityExcepted(vulnerability: Vulnerability, asset: Asset): Boolean {
    54→        val activeExceptions = getActiveExceptions()
    55→
    56→        // Check if any exception matches this vulnerability
    57→        return activeExceptions.any { exception ->
    58→            exception.matches(vulnerability, asset)
    59→        }
    60→    }
    61→
    62→    /**
    63→     * Find the matching exception for a vulnerability
    64→     *
    65→     * @param vulnerability The vulnerability to check
    66→     * @param asset The asset the vulnerability is on
    67→     * @return The matching exception, or null if none found
    68→     */
    69→    fun findMatchingException(vulnerability: Vulnerability, asset: Asset): VulnerabilityException? {
    70→        val activeExceptions = getActiveExceptions()
    71→
    72→        // Return the first matching exception
    73→        return activeExceptions.firstOrNull { exception ->
    74→            exception.matches(vulnerability, asset)
    75→        }
    76→    }
    77→
    78→    /**
    79→     * Get all exceptions with optional filters
    80→     *
    81→     * Feature 021 Phase 3: Now includes affected vulnerability counts
    82→     *
    83→     * @param activeOnly If true, only return active exceptions
    84→     * @param type Optional filter by exception type
    85→     * @param includeAffectedCount If true, calculate affected vulnerability count (more expensive)
    86→     * @return List of exception DTOs
    87→     */
    88→    fun getAllExceptions(
    89→        activeOnly: Boolean = false,
    90→        type: ExceptionType? = null,
    91→        includeAffectedCount: Boolean = true  // Feature 021 Phase 3: Default true
    92→    ): List<VulnerabilityExceptionDto> {
    93→        log.debug("Getting all exceptions - activeOnly: {}, type: {}, includeAffectedCount: {}",
    94→            activeOnly, type, includeAffectedCount)
    95→
    96→        val exceptions = when {
    97→            activeOnly && type != null -> {
    98→                getActiveExceptions().filter { it.exceptionType == type }
    99→            }
   100→            activeOnly -> {
   101→                getActiveExceptions()
   102→            }
   103→            type != null -> {
   104→                vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.findByExceptionType(type)
   105→            }
   106→            else -> {
   107→                vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.findAll().toList()
   108→            }
   109→        }
   110→
   111→        // Map to DTOs with optional affected count
   112→        return exceptions.map { exception ->
   113→            mapToDto(exception, includeAffectedCount)
   114→        }
   115→    }
   116→
   117→    /**
   118→     * Create a new vulnerability exception
   119→     *
   120→     * Feature 021: Now supports ASSET type with validation
   121→     *
   122→     * @param request The exception creation request
   123→     * @param username The username of the user creating the exception
   124→     * @return The created exception DTO
   125→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if ASSET exception with invalid assetId
   126→     */
   127→    fun createException(request: CreateVulnerabilityExceptionRequest, username: String): VulnerabilityExceptionDto {
   128→        log.info("Creating exception - type: {}, target: {}, assetId: {}, by: {}",
   129→            request.exceptionType, request.targetValue, request.assetId, username)
   130→
   131→        // Validate ASSET exception
   132→        if (request.exceptionType == ExceptionType.ASSET) {
   133→            if (request.assetId == null) {
   134→                throw IllegalArgumentException("Asset ID is required for ASSET-type exceptions")
   135→            }
   136→            // Verify asset exists
   137→            val asset = assetRepository.findById(request.assetId).orElse(null)
   138→                ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Asset not found with id: ${request.assetId}")
   139→
   140→            log.debug("ASSET exception validated for asset: {} ({})", asset.name, asset.id)
   141→        }
   142→
   143→        // Validate CVE exception (supports comma-separated CVE IDs)
   144→        if (request.exceptionType == ExceptionType.CVE) {
   145→            val cvePattern = Regex("^CVE-\\d{4}-\\d+$", RegexOption.IGNORE_CASE)
   146→            val cveIds = request.targetValue.split(",").map { it.trim() }.filter { it.isNotEmpty() }
   147→            if (cveIds.isEmpty()) {
   148→                throw IllegalArgumentException("At least one CVE ID is required")
   149→            }
   150→            for (cveId in cveIds) {
   151→                if (!cveId.matches(cvePattern)) {
   152→                    throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid CVE ID format: '$cveId'. Expected format: CVE-YYYY-NNNNN")
   153→                }
   154→            }
   155→            if (request.assetId != null) {
   156→                assetRepository.findById(request.assetId).orElse(null)
   157→                    ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Asset not found with id: ${request.assetId}")
   158→            }
   159→        }
   160→
   161→        // Normalize CVE targetValue: strip spaces around commas for FIND_IN_SET compatibility
   162→        val normalizedTargetValue = if (request.exceptionType == ExceptionType.CVE) {
   163→            request.targetValue.split(",").map { it.trim() }.filter { it.isNotEmpty() }.joinToString(",")
   164→        } else {
   165→            request.targetValue.trim()
   166→        }
   167→
   168→        val exception = VulnerabilityException(
   169→            exceptionType = request.exceptionType,
   170→            targetValue = normalizedTargetValue,
   171→            assetId = request.assetId,  // Feature 021: Set asset ID
   172→            expirationDate = request.expirationDate,
   173→            reason = request.reason.trim(),
   174→            createdBy = username
   175→        )
   176→
   177→        val saved = vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.save(exception)
   178→        log.info("Created exception with id: {}", saved.id)
   179→
   180→        return mapToDto(saved, includeAffectedCount = false) // Don't calculate on create (no vulns yet potentially)
   181→    }
   182→
   183→    /**
   184→     * Update an existing vulnerability exception
   185→     *
   186→     * Feature 021: Now supports updating ASSET type with validation
   187→     *
   188→     * @param id The exception ID to update
   189→     * @param request The exception update request
   190→     * @return The updated exception DTO
   191→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if exception not found or invalid asset ID
   192→     */
   193→    fun updateException(id: Long, request: UpdateVulnerabilityExceptionRequest): VulnerabilityExceptionDto {
   194→        log.info("Updating exception id: {}", id)
   195→
   196→        val exception = vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.findById(id).orElse(null)
   197→            ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Vulnerability exception not found with id: $id")
   198→
   199→        // Validate ASSET exception
   200→        if (request.exceptionType == ExceptionType.ASSET) {
   201→            if (request.assetId == null) {
   202→                throw IllegalArgumentException("Asset ID is required for ASSET-type exceptions")
   203→            }
   204→            // Verify asset exists
   205→            val asset = assetRepository.findById(request.assetId).orElse(null)
   206→                ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Asset not found with id: ${request.assetId}")
   207→
   208→            log.debug("ASSET exception validated for asset: {} ({})", asset.name, asset.id)
   209→        }
   210→
   211→        // Validate CVE exception (supports comma-separated CVE IDs)
   212→        if (request.exceptionType == ExceptionType.CVE) {
   213→            val cvePattern = Regex("^CVE-\\d{4}-\\d+$", RegexOption.IGNORE_CASE)
   214→            val cveIds = request.targetValue.split(",").map { it.trim() }.filter { it.isNotEmpty() }
   215→            if (cveIds.isEmpty()) {
   216→                throw IllegalArgumentException("At least one CVE ID is required")
   217→            }
   218→            for (cveId in cveIds) {
   219→                if (!cveId.matches(cvePattern)) {
   220→                    throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid CVE ID format: '$cveId'. Expected format: CVE-YYYY-NNNNN")
   221→                }
   222→            }
   223→            if (request.assetId != null) {
   224→                assetRepository.findById(request.assetId).orElse(null)
   225→                    ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Asset not found with id: ${request.assetId}")
   226→            }
   227→        }
   228→
   229→        exception.exceptionType = request.exceptionType
   230→        // Normalize CVE targetValue: strip spaces around commas for FIND_IN_SET compatibility
   231→        exception.targetValue = if (request.exceptionType == ExceptionType.CVE) {
   232→            request.targetValue.split(",").map { it.trim() }.filter { it.isNotEmpty() }.joinToString(",")
   233→        } else {
   234→            request.targetValue.trim()
   235→        }
   236→        exception.assetId = request.assetId  // Feature 021: Update asset ID
   237→        exception.expirationDate = request.expirationDate
   238→        exception.reason = request.reason.trim()
   239→
   240→        val updated = vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.update(exception)
   241→        log.info("Updated exception id: {}", id)
   242→
   243→        return mapToDto(updated, includeAffectedCount = false) // Don't calculate on update
   244→    }
   245→
   246→    /**
   247→     * Delete a vulnerability exception
   248→     *
   249→     * @param id The exception ID to delete
   250→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if exception not found
   251→     */
   252→    fun deleteException(id: Long) {
   253→        log.info("Deleting exception id: {}", id)
   254→
   255→        val exception = vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.findById(id).orElse(null)
   256→            ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Vulnerability exception not found with id: $id")
   257→
   258→        vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.deleteById(id)
   259→        log.info("Deleted exception id: {}", id)
   260→    }
   261→
   262→    /**
   263→     * Calculate how many vulnerabilities are affected by an exception
   264→     *
   265→     * Feature 021 Phase 3: Exception impact calculation
   266→     *
   267→     * @param exception The exception to check
   268→     * @return Count of affected vulnerabilities
   269→     */
   270→    fun calculateAffectedCount(exception: VulnerabilityException): Int {
   271→        return when (exception.exceptionType) {
   272→            ExceptionType.IP -> {
   273→                try {
   274→                    vulnerabilityRepository.countByAssetIp(exception.targetValue).toInt()
   275→                } catch (e: Exception) {
   276→                    log.warn("Failed to count IP-based exceptions: {}", e.message)
   277→                    0
   278→                }
   279→            }
   280→            ExceptionType.PRODUCT -> {
   281→                // Count vulnerabilities matching product pattern
   282→                try {
   283→                    vulnerabilityRepository.countByVulnerableProductVersionsContainingIgnoreCase(
   284→                        exception.targetValue
   285→                    ).toInt()
   286→                } catch (e: Exception) {
   287→                    log.warn("Failed to count product-based exceptions: {}", e.message)
   288→                    0
   289→                }
   290→            }
   291→            ExceptionType.ASSET -> {
   292→                // Count vulnerabilities for this specific asset
   293→                if (exception.assetId != null) {
   294→                    try {
   295→                        vulnerabilityRepository.countByAssetId(exception.assetId!!).toInt()
   296→                    } catch (e: Exception) {
   297→                        log.warn("Failed to count asset-based exceptions: {}", e.message)
   298→                        0
   299→                    }
   300→                } else {
   301→                    0
   302→                }
   303→            }
   304→            ExceptionType.CVE -> {
   305→                try {
   306→                    val cveIds = exception.targetValue.split(",").map { it.trim() }.filter { it.isNotEmpty() }
   307→                    if (exception.assetId != null) {
   308→                        vulnerabilityRepository.countByVulnerabilityIdInAndAssetId(cveIds, exception.assetId!!).toInt()
   309→                    } else {
   310→                        vulnerabilityRepository.countByVulnerabilityIdIn(cveIds).toInt()
   311→                    }
   312→                } catch (e: Exception) {
   313→                    log.warn("Failed to count CVE-based exceptions: {}", e.message)
   314→                    0
   315→                }
   316→            }
   317→        }
   318→    }
   319→
   320→    /**
   321→     * Map VulnerabilityException entity to DTO
   322→     *
   323→     * Feature 021: Now includes assetId, assetName, and optionally affectedCount
   324→     * Feature 021 Phase 3: Added affected count calculation
   325→     *
   326→     * @param exception The exception entity
   327→     * @param includeAffectedCount If true, calculate affected vulnerability count
   328→     * @return Exception DTO
   329→     */
   330→    private fun mapToDto(
   331→        exception: VulnerabilityException,
   332→        includeAffectedCount: Boolean = false
   333→    ): VulnerabilityExceptionDto {
   334→        // Fetch asset name if this is an ASSET or CVE exception with assetId
   335→        val assetName = if ((exception.exceptionType == ExceptionType.ASSET || exception.exceptionType == ExceptionType.CVE)
   336→            && exception.assetId != null) {
   337→            assetRepository.findById(exception.assetId!!).orElse(null)?.name
   338→        } else {
   339→            null
   340→        }
   341→
   342→        // Calculate affected count if requested
   343→        val affectedCount = if (includeAffectedCount) {
   344→            calculateAffectedCount(exception)
   345→        } else {
   346→            null
   347→        }
   348→        
   349→        return VulnerabilityExceptionDto(
   350→            id = exception.id,
   351→            exceptionType = exception.exceptionType,
   352→            targetValue = exception.targetValue,
   353→            expirationDate = exception.expirationDate,
   354→            reason = exception.reason,
   355→            createdBy = exception.createdBy,
   356→            createdAt = exception.createdAt,
   357→            updatedAt = exception.updatedAt,
   358→            isActive = exception.isActive(),
   359→            affectedVulnerabilityCount = affectedCount,  // Feature 021 Phase 3
   360→            assetId = exception.assetId,  // Feature 021
   361→            assetName = assetName  // Feature 021
   362→        )
   363→    }
   364→}
   365→

content.text:
Perfect! I've found and read all the relevant files for the vulnerability exception creation flow. Here's a comprehensive summary:

## Backend Files for Vulnerability Exception Creation Flow

### 1. **VulnerabilityExceptionRequestController.kt**
**Location:** `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/controller/VulnerabilityExceptionRequestController.kt`

This controller handles the exception **request** workflow (approval requests), not direct exception creation:
- `POST /api/vulnerability-exception-requests` - Create new exception request
- `GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/my` - Get user's requests
- `POST /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id}/approve` - Approve (ADMIN/SECCHAMPION)
- `POST /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id}/reject` - Reject (ADMIN/SECCHAMPION)
- Auto-approval logic: If requester has ADMIN/SECCHAMPION role, status becomes APPROVED immediately
- Concurrency control using optimistic locking (first-approver-wins)

### 2. **DTOs**

#### **VulnerabilityExceptionDto.kt**
**Location:** `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/dto/VulnerabilityExceptionDto.kt`

Response DTO for direct exceptions:
```kotlin
data class VulnerabilityExceptionDto(
    val id: Long?,
    val exceptionType: ExceptionType,  // IP, PRODUCT, ASSET, CVE
    val targetValue: String,
    val expirationDate: LocalDateTime?,
    val reason: String,
    val createdBy: String,
    val createdAt: LocalDateTime?,
    val updatedAt: LocalDateTime?,
    val isActive: Boolean,
    val affectedVulnerabilityCount: Int?,  // Count of affected vulns
    val assetId: Long?,  // Asset ID for ASSET-type exceptions
    val assetName: String?  // Asset name for display
)
```

Creation request DTO:
```kotlin
data class CreateVulnerabilityExceptionRequest(
    val exceptionType: ExceptionType,  // Required
    val targetValue: String,  // Required, max 512 chars
    val expirationDate: LocalDateTime?,
    val reason: String,  // Required, max 1024 chars
    val assetId: Long? = null  // For ASSET-type exceptions
)
```

#### **CreateExceptionRequestDto.kt** (for exception requests)
**Location:** `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/dto/CreateExceptionRequestDto.kt`

This is for **approval requests**, not direct exceptions:
```kotlin
data class CreateExceptionRequestDto(
    val vulnerabilityId: Long,  // Reference to specific vulnerability
    val scope: ExceptionScope,  // SINGLE_VULNERABILITY or CVE_PATTERN
    val reason: String,  // 50-2048 chars, business justification
    val expirationDate: LocalDateTime  // Must be future date
)
```

#### **VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto.kt**
**Location:** `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/dto/VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto.kt`

Response DTO for exception requests with denormalized vulnerability info:
- `vulnerabilityId`, `vulnerabilityCve`, `assetName`, `assetIp`
- `requestedByUsername`, `scope`, `reason`, `expirationDate`
- `status` (PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, EXPIRED, CANCELLED)
- `autoApproved`, `reviewedByUsername`, `reviewDate`, `reviewComment`

### 3. **VulnerabilityExceptionService.kt**
**Location:** `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/service/VulnerabilityExceptionService.kt`

Core service for direct exception management:

**Key Methods:**
- `createException()` - Creates new exception with validation
  - Validates ASSET exceptions: assetId required and must exist
  - Validates CVE exceptions: format CVE-YYYY-NNNNN, comma-separated supported
  - Normalizes CVE targetValue by stripping spaces around commas
  - Does NOT calculate affected count on creation
  
- `calculateAffectedCount(exception)` - Calculates affected vulnerabilities by type:
  - **IP**: `vulnerabilityRepository.countByAssetIp()`
  - **PRODUCT**: `vulnerabilityRepository.countByVulnerableProductVersionsContainingIgnoreCase()`
  - **ASSET**: `vulnerabilityRepository.countByAssetId()`
  - **CVE**: `vulnerabilityRepository.countByVulnerabilityIdIn()` or with assetId filter

- `updateException()` - Updates existing exception with same validation
- `deleteException()` - Deletes exception by ID
- `isVulnerabilityExcepted()` - Checks if vulnerability matches any active exception
- `findMatchingException()` - Returns first matching active exception
- `getAllExceptions()` - Lists all/active exceptions with optional affected counts

### 4. **VulnerabilityExceptionRepository.kt**
**Location:** `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/repository/VulnerabilityExceptionRepository.kt`

Data access layer methods:

Perfect! I've found and read all the relevant files for the vulnerability exception creation flow. Here's a comprehensive summary:

## Backend Files for Vulnerability Exception Creation Flow

### 1. **VulnerabilityExceptionRequestController.kt**
**Location:** `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/controller/VulnerabilityExceptionRequestController.kt`

This controller handles the exception **request** workflow (approval requests), not direct exception creation:
- `POST /api/vulnerability-exception-requests` - Create new exception request
- `GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/my` - Get user's requests
- `POST /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id}/approve` - Approve (ADMIN/SECCHAMPION)
- `POST /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id}/reject` - Reject (ADMIN/SECCHAMPION)
- Auto-approval logic: If requester has ADMIN/SECCHAMPION role, status becomes APPROVED immediately
- Concurrency control using optimistic locking (first-approver-wins)

### 2. **DTOs**

#### **VulnerabilityExceptionDto.kt**
**Location:** `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/dto/VulnerabilityExceptionDto.kt`

Response DTO for direct exceptions:
```kotlin
data class VulnerabilityExceptionDto(
    val id: Long?,
    val exceptionType: ExceptionType,  // IP, PRODUCT, ASSET, CVE
    val targetValue: String,
    val expirationDate: LocalDateTime?,
    val reason: String,
    val createdBy: String,
    val createdAt: LocalDateTime?,
    val updatedAt: LocalDateTime?,
    val isActive: Boolean,
    val affectedVulnerabilityCount: Int?,  // Count of affected vulns
    val assetId: Long?,  // Asset ID for ASSET-type exceptions
    val assetName: String?  // Asset name for display
)
```

Creation request DTO:
```kotlin
data class CreateVulnerabilityExceptionRequest(
    val exceptionType: ExceptionType,  // Required
    val targetValue: String,  // Required, max 512 chars
    val expirationDate: LocalDateTime?,
    val reason: String,  // Required, max 1024 chars
    val assetId: Long? = null  // For ASSET-type exceptions
)
```

#### **CreateExceptionRequestDto.kt** (for exception requests)
**Location:** `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/dto/CreateExceptionRequestDto.kt`

This is for **approval requests**, not direct exceptions:
```kotlin
data class CreateExceptionRequestDto(
    val vulnerabilityId: Long,  // Reference to specific vulnerability
    val scope: ExceptionScope,  // SINGLE_VULNERABILITY or CVE_PATTERN
    val reason: String,  // 50-2048 chars, business justification
    val expirationDate: LocalDateTime  // Must be future date
)
```

#### **VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto.kt**
**Location:** `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/dto/VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto.kt`

Response DTO for exception requests with denormalized vulnerability info:
- `vulnerabilityId`, `vulnerabilityCve`, `assetName`, `assetIp`
- `requestedByUsername`, `scope`, `reason`, `expirationDate`
- `status` (PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, EXPIRED, CANCELLED)
- `autoApproved`, `reviewedByUsername`, `reviewDate`, `reviewComment`

### 3. **VulnerabilityExceptionService.kt**
**Location:** `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/service/VulnerabilityExceptionService.kt`

Core service for direct exception management:

**Key Methods:**
- `createException()` - Creates new exception with validation
  - Validates ASSET exceptions: assetId required and must exist
  - Validates CVE exceptions: format CVE-YYYY-NNNNN, comma-separated supported
  - Normalizes CVE targetValue by stripping spaces around commas
  - Does NOT calculate affected count on creation
  
- `calculateAffectedCount(exception)` - Calculates affected vulnerabilities by type:
  - **IP**: `vulnerabilityRepository.countByAssetIp()`
  - **PRODUCT**: `vulnerabilityRepository.countByVulnerableProductVersionsContainingIgnoreCase()`
  - **ASSET**: `vulnerabilityRepository.countByAssetId()`
  - **CVE**: `vulnerabilityRepository.countByVulnerabilityIdIn()` or with assetId filter

- `updateException()` - Updates existing exception with same validation
- `deleteException()` - Deletes exception by ID
- `isVulnerabilityExcepted()` - Checks if vulnerability matches any active exception
- `findMatchingException()` - Returns first matching active exception
- `getAllExceptions()` - Lists all/active exceptions with optional affected counts

### 4. **VulnerabilityExceptionRepository.kt**
**Location:** `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/repository/VulnerabilityExceptionRepository.kt`

Data access layer methods:
- `findByExpirationDateIsNullOrExpirationDateGreaterThan()` - Find active (non-expired) exceptions
- `findByExceptionType()` - Filter by exception type
- `findByCreatedBy()` - Find exceptions created by specific user
- `findByExceptionTypeAndAssetId()` - Find ASSET-type exceptions for specific asset
- `findByExceptionTypeAndTargetValue()` - Find PRODUCT-type exceptions with target value

---

## Key Insights

1. **Two Parallel Workflows:**
   - **Direct Exceptions** (VulnerabilityExceptionService): Created by VULN/ADMIN roles directly
   - **Exception Requests** (VulnerabilityExceptionRequestService): Approval workflow for regular users

2. **Affected Count Calculation:** Only done when explicitly requested (expensive operation), not during creation

3. **CVE Exception Normalization:** Comma-separated CVEs are supported with automatic whitespace normalization

4. **ASSET Exception Validation:** Requires assetId to be provided and asset must exist in database

5. **Exception Types:** IP, PRODUCT, ASSET, CVE with type-specific matching logic

All files have been read in full and are located in the paths above.

**2. ExceptionRequestModal.tsx**
- Location: `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/frontend/src/components/ExceptionRequestModal.tsx`
- Modal for regular users to request exceptions (different from admin creation)
- Scope radio buttons: **SINGLE_VULNERABILITY** or **CVE_PATTERN**
- 50-2048 character reason validation
- Long expiration warning (>365 days) with confirmation modal
- API call: `createRequest()` to `/api/vulnerability-exception-requests`

**3. VulnerabilityExceptionsTable.tsx**
- Location: `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/frontend/src/components/VulnerabilityExceptionsTable.tsx`
- Displays list of all exceptions in table format

**4. ImpactPreviewModal.tsx**
- Location: `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/frontend/src/components/ImpactPreviewModal.tsx`
- Phase 3 preview feature showing vulnerability impact before creation
- Displays:

  - Breakdown by severity (CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW)
  - Sample of first 10 vulnerabilities
  - Warning if no vulnerabilities affected
  - Info about what happens next
- Confirm & Create vs Cancel actions

**5. ProductAutocomplete.tsx**
- Location: `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/frontend/src/components/ProductAutocomplete.tsx`
- Autocomplete input for PRODUCT exception type
- Fetches unique products from `/api/vulnerability-products`
- Filters as user types
- Shows up to 20 suggestions
- Highlights matching text
- Allows manual entry

### API Services

**vulnerabilityManagementService.ts**
- Location: `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/frontend/src/services/vulnerabilityManagementService.ts`
- Key functions:
  - `getVulnerabilityExceptions()` → GET `/api/vulnerability-exceptions`
  - `createException(request)` → POST `/api/vulnerability-exceptions`
  - `updateException(id, request)` → PUT `/api/vulnerability-exceptions/{id}`
  - `deleteException(id)` → DELETE `/api/vulnerability-exceptions/{id}`
  - `getAssets()` → GET `/api/assets` (returns `{ id, name, ip }`)
  - `previewExceptionImpact(request)` → POST `/api/vulnerability-exceptions/preview` (Phase 3)
  - `getDistinctProducts(search?, limit)` → GET `/api/vulnerability-products`

**exceptionRequestService.ts**
- Location: `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/frontend/src/services/exceptionRequestService.ts`
- For user-requested exceptions (approval workflow):
  - `createRequest(dto)` → POST `/api/vulnerability-exception-requests`
  - `getMyRequests()`, `getPendingRequests()`, `getStatistics()`, etc.

### Exception Type Details

The radio buttons render these types:

1. **IP Address**: Single IPv4 with validation (e.g., 192.168.1.100)
2. **Product**: Product name/version with autocomplete suggestions
3. **Asset**: Dropdown selection from available assets
4. **CVE**: Comma-separated CVE IDs with optional scope selection

### Form Submission Flow

1. User fills form and clicks "Preview & Create"
2. Form validates input
3. For new exceptions: `handlePreviewImpact()` calls `/api/vulnerability-exceptions/preview`
4. Impact preview modal displays affected vulnerabilities
5. User confirms in modal
6. `handleConfirmAndCreate()` calls `createException()` → POST `/api/vulnerability-exceptions`
7. For edit mode: Skips preview, goes straight to update

### Key Validation Rules

- **IP**: Valid IPv4 format (0-255 in each octet)
- **CVE**: Format `CVE-YYYY-NNNNN`, comma-separated, max 512 chars
- **Reason**: Required, max 1024 chars
- **Expiration**: Required, must be future date
- **Asset/CVE Scope**: Required when applicable

All components use Bootstrap 5.3 classes for styling and rely on the `authenticatedGet/Post/Put/Delete` utilities from `../utils/auth` for JWT-authenticated API calls.

content.content:
1→package com.secman.domain
     2→
     3→import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable
     4→import jakarta.persistence.*
     5→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotBlank
     6→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotNull
     7→import jakarta.validation.constraints.Size
     8→import java.time.LocalDateTime
     9→
    10→/**
    11→ * VulnerabilityException entity representing an exception rule for vulnerabilities.
    12→ *
    13→ * Exceptions can be:
    14→ * - IP-based: Applies to all vulnerabilities on a specific IP address
    15→ * - PRODUCT-based: Applies to all vulnerabilities matching a product pattern
    16→ * - ASSET-based: Applies to all vulnerabilities on a specific asset (by ID)
    17→ * - CVE-based: Applies to a specific CVE ID, optionally scoped to one asset
    18→ *   - targetValue = one or more comma-separated CVE IDs (e.g., "CVE-2023-29326,CVE-2024-1234")
    19→ *   - assetId = null for system-wide, or asset ID for single-system scope
    20→ *
    21→ * All exceptions require an expiration date (Feature 021).
    22→ * Time-limited exceptions enforce periodic review.
    23→ *
    24→ * Related to: Feature 004-i-want-to (VULN Role & Vulnerability Management UI)
    25→ * Related to: Feature 021-vulnerability-overdue-exception-logic (Asset exceptions)
    26→ */
    27→@Entity
    28→@Table(
    29→    name = "vulnerability_exception",
    30→    indexes = [
    31→        Index(name = "idx_vuln_exception_type", columnList = "exception_type"),
    32→        Index(name = "idx_vuln_exception_expiration", columnList = "expiration_date"),
    33→        Index(name = "idx_vuln_exception_asset", columnList = "asset_id"),
    34→        // PERFORMANCE: Composite indexes for faster exception matching queries
    35→        Index(name = "idx_vuln_exception_type_target", columnList = "exception_type, target_value"),
    36→        Index(name = "idx_vuln_exception_active", columnList = "expiration_date, exception_type")
    37→    ]
    38→)
    39→@Serdeable
    40→data class VulnerabilityException(
    41→    @Id
    42→    @GeneratedValue(strategy = GenerationType.IDENTITY)
    43→    var id: Long? = null,
    44→
    45→    /**
    46→     * Type of exception: IP, PRODUCT, ASSET, or CVE
    47→     */
    48→    @Enumerated(EnumType.STRING)
    49→    @Column(name = "exception_type", nullable = false, length = 20)
    50→    @NotNull
    51→    var exceptionType: ExceptionType,
    52→
    53→    /**
    54→     * Target value for the exception:
    55→     * - For IP: exact IP address (e.g., "192.168.1.10")
    56→     * - For PRODUCT: product name/version pattern (e.g., "OpenSSH 7.4")
    57→     * - For ASSET: not used (assetId field used instead)
    58→     * - For CVE: one or more comma-separated CVE identifiers (e.g., "CVE-2023-29326,CVE-2024-1234")
    59→     */
    60→    @Column(name = "target_value", nullable = false, length = 512)
    61→    @NotBlank
    62→    @Size(max = 512)
    63→    var targetValue: String,
    64→
    65→    /**
    66→     * Asset ID for ASSET-type and CVE-type exceptions
    67→     * - For IP/PRODUCT exceptions: null
    68→     * - For ASSET exceptions: ID of the specific asset
    69→     * - For CVE exceptions: null (system-wide) or asset ID (single-system scope)
    70→     */
    71→    @Column(name = "asset_id")
    72→    var assetId: Long? = null,
    73→
    74→    /**
    75→     * Expiration date for the exception
    76→     * Feature 021: All exceptions now require an end date
    77→     * - null = legacy exception (from before Feature 021)
    78→     * - non-null = exception expires after this date (required going forward)
    79→     */
    80→    @Column(name = "expiration_date")
    81→    var expirationDate: LocalDateTime? = null,
    82→
    83→    /**
    84→     * Reason/justification for the exception
    85→     */
    86→    @Column(name = "reason", nullable = false, length = 1024)
    87→    @NotBlank
    88→    @Size(max = 1024)
    89→    var reason: String,
    90→
    91→    /**
    92→     * User who created the exception
    93→     */
    94→    @Column(name = "created_by", nullable = false, length = 255)
    95→    @NotBlank
    96→    @Size(max = 255)
    97→    var createdBy: String,
    98→
    99→    @Column(name = "created_at", updatable = false)
   100→    var createdAt: LocalDateTime? = null,
   101→
   102→    @Column(name = "updated_at")
   103→    var updatedAt: LocalDateTime? = null
   104→) {
   105→    enum class ExceptionType {
   106→        IP,      // Exception applies to specific IP address
   107→        PRODUCT, // Exception applies to product name/version pattern
   108→        ASSET,   // Exception applies to specific asset by ID (Feature 021)
   109→        CVE      // Exception applies to specific CVE ID, optionally scoped to one asset
   110→    }
   111→
   112→    @PrePersist
   113→    fun onCreate() {
   114→        val now = LocalDateTime.now()
   115→        createdAt = now
   116→        updatedAt = now
   117→    }
   118→
   119→    @PreUpdate
   120→    fun onUpdate() {
   121→        updatedAt = LocalDateTime.now()
   122→    }
   123→
   124→    /**
   125→     * Check if this exception is currently active
   126→     */
   127→    fun isActive(): Boolean {
   128→        return expirationDate == null || expirationDate!!.isAfter(LocalDateTime.now())
   129→    }
   130→
   131→    /**
   132→     * Check if this exception matches the given vulnerability and asset
   133→     *
   134→     * Feature 021: Added ASSET type matching by asset ID
   135→     * Bug fix: PRODUCT type now also matches CVE ID patterns
   136→     */
   137→    fun matches(vulnerability: Vulnerability, asset: Asset): Boolean {
   138→        if (!isActive()) return false
   139→
   140→        return when (exceptionType) {
   141→            ExceptionType.IP -> asset.ip == targetValue
   142→            ExceptionType.PRODUCT -> {
   143→                // Match either CVE ID (e.g., "CVE-2023-29326") or product version string
   144→                vulnerability.vulnerabilityId == targetValue ||
   145→                vulnerability.vulnerableProductVersions?.contains(targetValue) == true
   146→            }
   147→            ExceptionType.ASSET -> assetId != null && assetId == asset.id
   148→            ExceptionType.CVE -> {
   149→                val cveIds = targetValue.split(",").map { it.trim() }
   150→                val cveMatches = vulnerability.vulnerabilityId in cveIds
   151→                if (assetId != null) {
   152→                    cveMatches && assetId == asset.id
   153→                } else {
   154→                    cveMatches
   155→                }
   156→            }
   157→        }
   158→    }
   159→
   160→    override fun toString(): String {
   161→        return "VulnerabilityException(id=$id, type=$exceptionType, target='$targetValue', " +
   162→                "assetId=$assetId, expires=${expirationDate ?: "never"}, active=${isActive()})"
   163→    }
   164→
   165→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean {
   166→        if (this === other) return true
   167→        if (other !is VulnerabilityException) return false
   168→        return id != null && id == other.id
   169→    }
   170→
   171→    override fun hashCode(): Int {
   172→        return id?.hashCode() ?: 0
   173→    }
   174→}
   175→

1→package com.secman.service
     2→
     3→import com.secman.domain.Asset
     4→import com.secman.domain.Vulnerability
     5→import com.secman.domain.VulnerabilityException
     6→import com.secman.domain.VulnerabilityException.ExceptionType
     7→import com.secman.dto.CreateVulnerabilityExceptionRequest
     8→import com.secman.dto.UpdateVulnerabilityExceptionRequest
     9→import com.secman.dto.VulnerabilityExceptionDto
    10→import com.secman.repository.AssetRepository
    11→import com.secman.repository.VulnerabilityExceptionRepository
    12→import com.secman.repository.VulnerabilityRepository
    13→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
    14→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    15→import java.time.LocalDateTime
    16→
    17→/**
    18→ * Service for vulnerability exception management
    19→ *
    20→ * Handles:
    21→ * - Creating, updating, deleting exceptions (IP, PRODUCT, ASSET, CVE types)
    22→ * - Checking if vulnerabilities are excepted
    23→ * - Managing exception lifecycle (active/expired)
    24→ *
    25→ * Related to: Feature 004-i-want-to (VULN Role & Vulnerability Management UI)
    26→ * Related to: Feature 021-vulnerability-overdue-exception-logic (Added ASSET support)
    27→ */
    28→@Singleton
    29→class VulnerabilityExceptionService(
    30→    private val vulnerabilityExceptionRepository: VulnerabilityExceptionRepository,
    31→    private val assetRepository: AssetRepository,
    32→    private val vulnerabilityRepository: VulnerabilityRepository  // Feature 021 Phase 3: For affected counts
    33→) {
    34→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(VulnerabilityExceptionService::class.java)
    35→
    36→    /**
    37→     * Get all active exceptions (not expired)
    38→     *
    39→     * @return List of active vulnerability exceptions
    40→     */
    41→    fun getActiveExceptions(): List<VulnerabilityException> {
    42→        val now = LocalDateTime.now()
    43→        return vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.findByExpirationDateIsNullOrExpirationDateGreaterThan(now)
    44→    }
    45→
    46→    /**
    47→     * Check if a vulnerability is excepted
    48→     *
    49→     * @param vulnerability The vulnerability to check
    50→     * @param asset The asset the vulnerability is on
    51→     * @return True if vulnerability is excepted, false otherwise
    52→     */
    53→    fun isVulnerabilityExcepted(vulnerability: Vulnerability, asset: Asset): Boolean {
    54→        val activeExceptions = getActiveExceptions()
    55→
    56→        // Check if any exception matches this vulnerability
    57→        return activeExceptions.any { exception ->
    58→            exception.matches(vulnerability, asset)
    59→        }
    60→    }
    61→
    62→    /**
    63→     * Find the matching exception for a vulnerability
    64→     *
    65→     * @param vulnerability The vulnerability to check
    66→     * @param asset The asset the vulnerability is on
    67→     * @return The matching exception, or null if none found
    68→     */
    69→    fun findMatchingException(vulnerability: Vulnerability, asset: Asset): VulnerabilityException? {
    70→        val activeExceptions = getActiveExceptions()
    71→
    72→        // Return the first matching exception
    73→        return activeExceptions.firstOrNull { exception ->
    74→            exception.matches(vulnerability, asset)
    75→        }
    76→    }
    77→
    78→    /**
    79→     * Get all exceptions with optional filters
    80→     *
    81→     * Feature 021 Phase 3: Now includes affected vulnerability counts
    82→     *
    83→     * @param activeOnly If true, only return active exceptions
    84→     * @param type Optional filter by exception type
    85→     * @param includeAffectedCount If true, calculate affected vulnerability count (more expensive)
    86→     * @return List of exception DTOs
    87→     */
    88→    fun getAllExceptions(
    89→        activeOnly: Boolean = false,
    90→        type: ExceptionType? = null,
    91→        includeAffectedCount: Boolean = true  // Feature 021 Phase 3: Default true
    92→    ): List<VulnerabilityExceptionDto> {
    93→        log.debug("Getting all exceptions - activeOnly: {}, type: {}, includeAffectedCount: {}",
    94→            activeOnly, type, includeAffectedCount)
    95→
    96→        val exceptions = when {
    97→            activeOnly && type != null -> {
    98→                getActiveExceptions().filter { it.exceptionType == type }
    99→            }
   100→            activeOnly -> {
   101→                getActiveExceptions()
   102→            }
   103→            type != null -> {
   104→                vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.findByExceptionType(type)
   105→            }
   106→            else -> {
   107→                vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.findAll().toList()
   108→            }
   109→        }
   110→
   111→        // Map to DTOs with optional affected count
   112→        return exceptions.map { exception ->
   113→            mapToDto(exception, includeAffectedCount)
   114→        }
   115→    }
   116→
   117→    /**
   118→     * Create a new vulnerability exception
   119→     *
   120→     * Feature 021: Now supports ASSET type with validation
   121→     *
   122→     * @param request The exception creation request
   123→     * @param username The username of the user creating the exception
   124→     * @return The created exception DTO
   125→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if ASSET exception with invalid assetId
   126→     */
   127→    fun createException(request: CreateVulnerabilityExceptionRequest, username: String): VulnerabilityExceptionDto {
   128→        log.info("Creating exception - type: {}, target: {}, assetId: {}, by: {}",
   129→            request.exceptionType, request.targetValue, request.assetId, username)
   130→
   131→        // Validate ASSET exception
   132→        if (request.exceptionType == ExceptionType.ASSET) {
   133→            if (request.assetId == null) {
   134→                throw IllegalArgumentException("Asset ID is required for ASSET-type exceptions")
   135→            }
   136→            // Verify asset exists
   137→            val asset = assetRepository.findById(request.assetId).orElse(null)
   138→                ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Asset not found with id: ${request.assetId}")
   139→
   140→            log.debug("ASSET exception validated for asset: {} ({})", asset.name, asset.id)
   141→        }
   142→
   143→        // Validate CVE exception (supports comma-separated CVE IDs)
   144→        if (request.exceptionType == ExceptionType.CVE) {
   145→            val cvePattern = Regex("^CVE-\\d{4}-\\d+$", RegexOption.IGNORE_CASE)
   146→            val cveIds = request.targetValue.split(",").map { it.trim() }.filter { it.isNotEmpty() }
   147→            if (cveIds.isEmpty()) {
   148→                throw IllegalArgumentException("At least one CVE ID is required")
   149→            }
   150→            for (cveId in cveIds) {
   151→                if (!cveId.matches(cvePattern)) {
   152→                    throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid CVE ID format: '$cveId'. Expected format: CVE-YYYY-NNNNN")
   153→                }
   154→            }
   155→            if (request.assetId != null) {
   156→                assetRepository.findById(request.assetId).orElse(null)
   157→                    ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Asset not found with id: ${request.assetId}")
   158→            }
   159→        }
   160→
   161→        // Normalize CVE targetValue: strip spaces around commas for FIND_IN_SET compatibility
   162→        val normalizedTargetValue = if (request.exceptionType == ExceptionType.CVE) {
   163→            request.targetValue.split(",").map { it.trim() }.filter { it.isNotEmpty() }.joinToString(",")
   164→        } else {
   165→            request.targetValue.trim()
   166→        }
   167→
   168→        val exception = VulnerabilityException(
   169→            exceptionType = request.exceptionType,
   170→            targetValue = normalizedTargetValue,
   171→            assetId = request.assetId,  // Feature 021: Set asset ID
   172→            expirationDate = request.expirationDate,
   173→            reason = request.reason.trim(),
   174→            createdBy = username
   175→        )
   176→
   177→        val saved = vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.save(exception)
   178→        log.info("Created exception with id: {}", saved.id)
   179→
   180→        return mapToDto(saved, includeAffectedCount = false) // Don't calculate on create (no vulns yet potentially)
   181→    }
   182→
   183→    /**
   184→     * Update an existing vulnerability exception
   185→     *
   186→     * Feature 021: Now supports updating ASSET type with validation
   187→     *
   188→     * @param id The exception ID to update
   189→     * @param request The exception update request
   190→     * @return The updated exception DTO
   191→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if exception not found or invalid asset ID
   192→     */
   193→    fun updateException(id: Long, request: UpdateVulnerabilityExceptionRequest): VulnerabilityExceptionDto {
   194→        log.info("Updating exception id: {}", id)
   195→
   196→        val exception = vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.findById(id).orElse(null)
   197→            ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Vulnerability exception not found with id: $id")
   198→
   199→        // Validate ASSET exception
   200→        if (request.exceptionType == ExceptionType.ASSET) {
   201→            if (request.assetId == null) {
   202→                throw IllegalArgumentException("Asset ID is required for ASSET-type exceptions")
   203→            }
   204→            // Verify asset exists
   205→            val asset = assetRepository.findById(request.assetId).orElse(null)
   206→                ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Asset not found with id: ${request.assetId}")
   207→
   208→            log.debug("ASSET exception validated for asset: {} ({})", asset.name, asset.id)
   209→        }
   210→
   211→        // Validate CVE exception (supports comma-separated CVE IDs)
   212→        if (request.exceptionType == ExceptionType.CVE) {
   213→            val cvePattern = Regex("^CVE-\\d{4}-\\d+$", RegexOption.IGNORE_CASE)
   214→            val cveIds = request.targetValue.split(",").map { it.trim() }.filter { it.isNotEmpty() }
   215→            if (cveIds.isEmpty()) {
   216→                throw IllegalArgumentException("At least one CVE ID is required")
   217→            }
   218→            for (cveId in cveIds) {
   219→                if (!cveId.matches(cvePattern)) {
   220→                    throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid CVE ID format: '$cveId'. Expected format: CVE-YYYY-NNNNN")
   221→                }
   222→            }
   223→            if (request.assetId != null) {
   224→                assetRepository.findById(request.assetId).orElse(null)
   225→                    ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Asset not found with id: ${request.assetId}")
   226→            }
   227→        }
   228→
   229→        exception.exceptionType = request.exceptionType
   230→        // Normalize CVE targetValue: strip spaces around commas for FIND_IN_SET compatibility
   231→        exception.targetValue = if (request.exceptionType == ExceptionType.CVE) {
   232→            request.targetValue.split(",").map { it.trim() }.filter { it.isNotEmpty() }.joinToString(",")
   233→        } else {
   234→            request.targetValue.trim()
   235→        }
   236→        exception.assetId = request.assetId  // Feature 021: Update asset ID
   237→        exception.expirationDate = request.expirationDate
   238→        exception.reason = request.reason.trim()
   239→
   240→        val updated = vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.update(exception)
   241→        log.info("Updated exception id: {}", id)
   242→
   243→        return mapToDto(updated, includeAffectedCount = false) // Don't calculate on update
   244→    }
   245→
   246→    /**
   247→     * Delete a vulnerability exception
   248→     *
   249→     * @param id The exception ID to delete
   250→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if exception not found
   251→     */
   252→    fun deleteException(id: Long) {
   253→        log.info("Deleting exception id: {}", id)
   254→
   255→        val exception = vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.findById(id).orElse(null)
   256→            ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Vulnerability exception not found with id: $id")
   257→
   258→        vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.deleteById(id)
   259→        log.info("Deleted exception id: {}", id)
   260→    }
   261→
   262→    /**
   263→     * Calculate how many vulnerabilities are affected by an exception
   264→     *
   265→     * Feature 021 Phase 3: Exception impact calculation
   266→     *
   267→     * @param exception The exception to check
   268→     * @return Count of affected vulnerabilities
   269→     */
   270→    fun calculateAffectedCount(exception: VulnerabilityException): Int {
   271→        return when (exception.exceptionType) {
   272→            ExceptionType.IP -> {
   273→                try {
   274→                    vulnerabilityRepository.countByAssetIp(exception.targetValue).toInt()
   275→                } catch (e: Exception) {
   276→                    log.warn("Failed to count IP-based exceptions: {}", e.message)
   277→                    0
   278→                }
   279→            }
   280→            ExceptionType.PRODUCT -> {
   281→                // Count vulnerabilities matching product pattern
   282→                try {
   283→                    vulnerabilityRepository.countByVulnerableProductVersionsContainingIgnoreCase(
   284→                        exception.targetValue
   285→                    ).toInt()
   286→                } catch (e: Exception) {
   287→                    log.warn("Failed to count product-based exceptions: {}", e.message)
   288→                    0
   289→                }
   290→            }
   291→            ExceptionType.ASSET -> {
   292→                // Count vulnerabilities for this specific asset
   293→                if (exception.assetId != null) {
   294→                    try {
   295→                        vulnerabilityRepository.countByAssetId(exception.assetId!!).toInt()
   296→                    } catch (e: Exception) {
   297→                        log.warn("Failed to count asset-based exceptions: {}", e.message)
   298→                        0
   299→                    }
   300→                } else {
   301→                    0
   302→                }
   303→            }
   304→            ExceptionType.CVE -> {
   305→                try {
   306→                    val cveIds = exception.targetValue.split(",").map { it.trim() }.filter { it.isNotEmpty() }
   307→                    if (exception.assetId != null) {
   308→                        vulnerabilityRepository.countByVulnerabilityIdInAndAssetId(cveIds, exception.assetId!!).toInt()
   309→                    } else {
   310→                        vulnerabilityRepository.countByVulnerabilityIdIn(cveIds).toInt()
   311→                    }
   312→                } catch (e: Exception) {
   313→                    log.warn("Failed to count CVE-based exceptions: {}", e.message)
   314→                    0
   315→                }
   316→            }
   317→        }
   318→    }
   319→
   320→    /**
   321→     * Map VulnerabilityException entity to DTO
   322→     *
   323→     * Feature 021: Now includes assetId, assetName, and optionally affectedCount
   324→     * Feature 021 Phase 3: Added affected count calculation
   325→     *
   326→     * @param exception The exception entity
   327→     * @param includeAffectedCount If true, calculate affected vulnerability count
   328→     * @return Exception DTO
   329→     */
   330→    private fun mapToDto(
   331→        exception: VulnerabilityException,
   332→        includeAffectedCount: Boolean = false
   333→    ): VulnerabilityExceptionDto {
   334→        // Fetch asset name if this is an ASSET or CVE exception with assetId
   335→        val assetName = if ((exception.exceptionType == ExceptionType.ASSET || exception.exceptionType == ExceptionType.CVE)
   336→            && exception.assetId != null) {
   337→            assetRepository.findById(exception.assetId!!).orElse(null)?.name
   338→        } else {
   339→            null
   340→        }
   341→
   342→        // Calculate affected count if requested
   343→        val affectedCount = if (includeAffectedCount) {
   344→            calculateAffectedCount(exception)
   345→        } else {
   346→            null
   347→        }
   348→        
   349→        return VulnerabilityExceptionDto(
   350→            id = exception.id,
   351→            exceptionType = exception.exceptionType,
   352→            targetValue = exception.targetValue,
   353→            expirationDate = exception.expirationDate,
   354→            reason = exception.reason,
   355→            createdBy = exception.createdBy,
   356→            createdAt = exception.createdAt,
   357→            updatedAt = exception.updatedAt,
   358→            isActive = exception.isActive(),
   359→            affectedVulnerabilityCount = affectedCount,  // Feature 021 Phase 3
   360→            assetId = exception.assetId,  // Feature 021
   361→            assetName = assetName  // Feature 021
   362→        )
   363→    }
   364→}
   365→

package com.secman.repository

import com.secman.domain.VulnerabilityException
import com.secman.domain.VulnerabilityException.ExceptionType
import io.micronaut.data.annotation.Repository
import io.micronaut.data.jpa.repository.JpaRepository
import java.time.LocalDateTime

/**
 * Repository for VulnerabilityException entity operations
 *
 * Related to: Feature 004-i-want-to (VULN Role & Vulnerability Management UI)
 */
@Repository
interface VulnerabilityExceptionRepository : JpaRepository<VulnerabilityException, Long> {

    /**
     * Find all active exceptions (not expired)
     * Used for checking if vulnerabilities are excepted
     *
     * @param date The current date/time to compare against expiration
     * @return List of active exceptions
     */
    fun findByExpirationDateIsNullOrExpirationDateGreaterThan(date: LocalDateTime): List<VulnerabilityException>

    /**
     * Find exceptions by type (IP or PRODUCT)
     *
     * @param type The exception type to filter by
     * @return List of exceptions matching the type
     */
    fun findByExceptionType(type: ExceptionType): List<VulnerabilityException>

    /**
     * Find exceptions created by a specific user
     *
     * @param username The username who created the exceptions
     * @return List of exceptions created by the user
     */
    fun findByCreatedBy(username: String): List<VulnerabilityException>

    /**
     * Find exceptions by type and asset ID.
     *
     * Used by expiration scheduler to find ASSET-type exceptions for deactivation.
     *
     * @param type Exception type (typically ASSET)
     * @param assetId Asset ID to match
     * @return List of exceptions matching criteria
     */
    fun findByExceptionTypeAndAssetId(
        type: ExceptionType,
        assetId: Long
    ): List<VulnerabilityException>

    /**
     * Find exceptions by type and target value.
     *
     * Used by expiration scheduler to find PRODUCT-type exceptions for deactivation.
     * Target value typically contains CVE ID for PRODUCT-type exceptions.
     *
     * @param type Exception type (typically PRODUCT)
     * @param targetValue Target value to match (e.g., CVE ID)
     * @return List of exceptions matching criteria
     */
    fun findByExceptionTypeAndTargetValue(
        type: ExceptionType,
        targetValue: String
    ): List<VulnerabilityException>
}

1→package com.secman.dto
     2→
     3→import com.fasterxml.jackson.annotation.JsonIgnore
     4→import com.secman.domain.VulnerabilityException.ExceptionType
     5→import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable
     6→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotBlank
     7→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotNull
     8→import jakarta.validation.constraints.Size
     9→import java.time.LocalDateTime
    10→
    11→/**
    12→ * DTO for VulnerabilityException response
    13→ *
    14→ * Related to: Feature 004-i-want-to (VULN Role & Vulnerability Management UI)
    15→ * Related to: Feature 021-vulnerability-overdue-exception-logic (Added assetId, assetName)
    16→ */
    17→@Serdeable
    18→data class VulnerabilityExceptionDto(
    19→    val id: Long?,
    20→    val exceptionType: ExceptionType,
    21→    val targetValue: String,
    22→    val expirationDate: LocalDateTime?,
    23→    val reason: String,
    24→    val createdBy: String,
    25→    val createdAt: LocalDateTime?,
    26→    val updatedAt: LocalDateTime?,
    27→    val isActive: Boolean,
    28→    val affectedVulnerabilityCount: Int? = null,  // Optional: for Exceptions list view
    29→    // Feature 021: Asset exception fields
    30→    val assetId: Long? = null,  // Asset ID for ASSET-type exceptions
    31→    val assetName: String? = null  // Asset name for display (populated if available)
    32→)
    33→
    34→/**
    35→ * Request DTO for creating a new VulnerabilityException
    36→ *
    37→ * Related to: Feature 004-i-want-to (VULN Role & Vulnerability Management UI)
    38→ * Related to: Feature 021-vulnerability-overdue-exception-logic (Added assetId)
    39→ */
    40→@Serdeable
    41→data class CreateVulnerabilityExceptionRequest(
    42→    @field:NotNull
    43→    val exceptionType: ExceptionType,
    44→
    45→    @field:NotBlank
    46→    @field:Size(max = 512)
    47→    val targetValue: String,
    48→
    49→    val expirationDate: LocalDateTime?,
    50→
    51→    @field:NotBlank
    52→    @field:Size(max = 1024)
    53→    val reason: String,
    54→    
    55→    // Feature 021: Asset ID for ASSET-type exceptions
    56→    val assetId: Long? = null
    57→)
    58→
    59→/**
    60→ * Request DTO for updating an existing VulnerabilityException
    61→ *
    62→ * Related to: Feature 004-i-want-to (VULN Role & Vulnerability Management UI)
    63→ * Related to: Feature 021-vulnerability-overdue-exception-logic (Added assetId)
    64→ */
    65→@Serdeable
    66→data class UpdateVulnerabilityExceptionRequest(
    67→    @field:NotNull
    68→    val exceptionType: ExceptionType,
    69→
    70→    @field:NotBlank
    71→    @field:Size(max = 512)
    72→    val targetValue: String,
    73→
    74→    val expirationDate: LocalDateTime?,
    75→
    76→    @field:NotBlank
    77→    @field:Size(max = 1024)
    78→    val reason: String,
    79→    
    80→    // Feature 021: Asset ID for ASSET-type exceptions
    81→    val assetId: Long? = null
    82→)
    83→
    84→/**
    85→ * DTO for Vulnerability with exception information and overdue status
    86→ *
    87→ * Note: The `asset` field is excluded from JSON serialization (@JsonIgnore) to reduce
    88→ * API response sizes by ~40%. Use the flat fields (assetId, assetName, assetIp) for display.
    89→ * The asset field is retained for service-layer use (exception matching, etc.).
    90→ *
    91→ * Feature 004: Added exception information
    92→ * Feature 021: Added overdue status tracking
    93→ * Feature 073: Excluded asset from JSON serialization to reduce response size
    94→ *
    95→ * Related to: Feature 021-vulnerability-overdue-exception-logic
    96→ * Related to: Feature 073-memory-optimization
    97→ */
    98→@Serdeable
    99→data class VulnerabilityWithExceptionDto(

file.content:
package com.secman.dto

import com.fasterxml.jackson.annotation.JsonIgnore
import com.secman.domain.VulnerabilityException.ExceptionType
import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable
import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotBlank
import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotNull
import jakarta.validation.constraints.Size
import java.time.LocalDateTime

/**
 * DTO for VulnerabilityException response
 *
 * Related to: Feature 004-i-want-to (VULN Role & Vulnerability Management UI)
 * Related to: Feature 021-vulnerability-overdue-exception-logic (Added assetId, assetName)
 */
@Serdeable
data class VulnerabilityExceptionDto(
    val id: Long?,
    val exceptionType: ExceptionType,
    val targetValue: String,
    val expirationDate: LocalDateTime?,
    val reason: String,
    val createdBy: String,
    val createdAt: LocalDateTime?,
    val updatedAt: LocalDateTime?,
    val isActive: Boolean,
    val affectedVulnerabilityCount: Int? = null,  // Optional: for Exceptions list view
    // Feature 021: Asset exception fields
    val assetId: Long? = null,  // Asset ID for ASSET-type exceptions
    val assetName: String? = null  // Asset name for display (populated if available)
)

/**
 * Request DTO for creating a new VulnerabilityException
 *
 * Related to: Feature 004-i-want-to (VULN Role & Vulnerability Management UI)
 * Related to: Feature 021-vulnerability-overdue-exception-logic (Added assetId)
 */
@Serdeable
data class CreateVulnerabilityExceptionRequest(
    @field:NotNull
    val exceptionType: ExceptionType,

    @field:NotBlank
    @field:Size(max = 512)
    val targetValue: String,

    val expirationDate: LocalDateTime?,

    @field:NotBlank
    @field:Size(max = 1024)
    val reason: String,
    
    // Feature 021: Asset ID for ASSET-type exceptions
    val assetId: Long? = null
)

/**
 * Request DTO for updating an existing VulnerabilityException
 *
 * Related to: Feature 004-i-want-to (VULN Role & Vulnerability Management UI)
 * Related to: Feature 021-vulnerability-overdue-exception-logic (Added assetId)
 */
@Serdeable
data class UpdateVulnerabilityExceptionRequest(
    @field:NotNull
    val exceptionType: ExceptionType,

    @field:NotBlank
    @field:Size(max = 512)
    val targetValue: String,

    val expirationDate: LocalDateTime?,

    @field:NotBlank
    @field:Size(max = 1024)
    val reason: String,
    
    // Feature 021: Asset ID for ASSET-type exceptions
    val assetId: Long? = null
)

/**
 * DTO for Vulnerability with exception information and overdue status
 *
 * Note: The `asset` field is excluded from JSON serialization (@JsonIgnore) to reduce
 * API response sizes by ~40%. Use the flat fields (assetId, assetName, assetIp) for display.
 * The asset field is retained for service-layer use (exception matching, etc.).
 *
 * Feature 004: Added exception information
 * Feature 021: Added overdue status tracking
 * Feature 073: Excluded asset from JSON serialization to reduce response size
 *
 * Related to: Feature 021-vulnerability-overdue-exception-logic
 * Related to: Feature 073-memory-optimization
 */
@Serdeable
data class VulnerabilityWithExceptionDto(
    val id: Long?,
    @field:JsonIgnore
    val asset: com.secman.domain.Asset,  // Excluded from JSON - use flat fields for API responses
    val assetId: Long,
    val assetName: String,
    val assetIp: String?,
    val vulnerabilityId: String?,
    val cvssSeverity: String?,
    val vulnerableProductVersions: String?,
    val daysOpen: String?,
    val scanTimestamp: LocalDateTime,
    val hasException: Boolean,  // Indicates if vulnerability is covered by active exception
    val exceptionReason: String? = null,  // Reason for exception (if applicable)
    
    // Feature 021: Overdue status fields
    val ageInDays: Int = 0,  // Age of vulnerability in days since scanTimestamp
    val overdueStatus: OverdueStatus = OverdueStatus.OK,  // Overdue status (OK/OVERDUE/EXCEPTED)
    val daysOverdue: Int? = null,  // Days past the threshold (null if not overdue)
    val exceptionId: Long? = null,  // ID of the exception (if excepted)
    val exceptionEndDate: LocalDateTime? = null  // Exception expiration date (if excepted)
)

/**
 * Paginated response wrapper for vulnerabilities
 *
 * Contains pagination metadata along with the vulnerability data
 */
@Serdeable
data class PaginatedVulnerabilitiesResponse(
    val content: List<VulnerabilityWithExceptionDto>,
    val totalElements: Long,
    val totalPages: Int,
    val currentPage: Int,
    val pageSize: Int,
    val hasNext: Boolean,
    val hasPrevious: Boolean
)

38-/**
39- * Controller for vulnerability management and exception operations
40- *
41- * Endpoints:
42- * - GET /api/vulnerabilities/current - List current vulnerabilities with filters
43: * - GET /api/vulnerability-exceptions - List vulnerability exceptions
44: * - POST /api/vulnerability-exceptions - Create new exception
45: * - PUT /api/vulnerability-exceptions/{id} - Update exception
46: * - DELETE /api/vulnerability-exceptions/{id} - Delete exception
47- *
48- * Access: ADMIN, VULN, and SECCHAMPION roles only
49- * Feature: 025-role-based-access-control
50- *
51- * Related to: Feature 004-i-want-to (VULN Role & Vulnerability Management UI)
--
